t_ch: 罢了，不重要…这些钱给你，从我眼前滚开吧！必须让你知道…我，伟大的艾默里奇，才不是什么守财奴…给我记住！
t_en: Fine, it doesn't matter anymore... Here, take the money, and get out of my sight! Just remember that I, the great Emmrich... I'm no miser... Keep that in mind!

t_ch: 艾默里奇陷入失落之中。虽然有些于心不忍，但你觉得让他保持这种状态对整个匹诺康尼而言是件好事。
t_en: Emmrich falls into a state of despair. While this is somewhat disheartening, keeping him in this state may be advantageous for the welfare of Penacony.

t_ch: 为什么…为什么明明我发明出了那么多好东西，大家还是不重视我？
t_en: Why... Why is it that people still don't take me seriously, even with all the awesome stuff I've invented?

t_ch: 我的要求明明不高…我只希望能得到尊重！当然，最好还能多赚点钱……
t_en: I'm not asking for much... All I want is a bit of respect! And, of course, some money, if possible...

t_ch: 啊？我没听错吧？
t_en: Huh? Did I hear it wrong!?

t_ch: 我能把你头上的灰毛都拔光吗？那样你就可以拥有一颗洁白无瑕、魅力四射的光头了——
t_en: Can I pull out all the gray hair on your head? That way, you'll have a pure and pristine bald head that exudes charm...

t_ch: ——当然不行，你这无礼的家伙！
t_en: ...Of course not! Rude!

t_ch: 你还好吗？
t_en: Are you okay?

t_ch: 眼前这男人好似一株刚淋过雨的香菇——奇妙的比喻，你大概是饿了——扎根于潮乎乎的颓丧，披戴着湿漉漉的愁苦。
t_en: The man in front of you looks like a wet mushroom freshly showered in rain, rooted in sogginess and sadness, robed in gloominess. Weird analogy, maybe you are just hungry?

t_ch: 不要试图与这样的人交谈，除非你实在无处打发时间。
t_en: Don't bother talking to him unless you're desperate for something to do.

t_ch: 还是不打扰他了吧。
t_en: It's best to just leave him be.

t_ch: 男人似乎感受到了你的视线，架在他脖子上的沉重包袱缓缓抬起。
t_en: The man senses your gaze. The weight on his shoulders lifts slightly.

t_ch: 你对上他的眼睛——浑浊、红肿、血丝遍布，好似两汪泥泞不堪的水坑。
t_en: You meet his bloodshot eyes, all hazy and muddy like puddles.

t_ch: …有什么事吗？
t_en: ...Can I help you?

t_ch: 你怎么了？
t_en: What's wrong?

t_ch: 我…怎么了？
t_en: What's wrong... with me?

t_ch: 男人的声音十分沙哑，这在遍地苏乐达的美梦中并不多见。他瞥了一眼身旁的拼图架。
t_en: The guy's voice is hoarse, which is pretty rare in this sweet dream filled with SoulGlad. He glances at the puzzle rack next to him.

t_ch: 是啊，我到底怎么了？我…怎么会爱上一条猎犬呢？
t_en: Yeah, what's wrong with me? How did I... end up falling for a Bloodhound?

t_ch: 这是比喻，对吧？
t_en: It's a metaphor, right?

t_ch: …当然是比喻，否则呢？
t_en: ...Of course it's a metaphor. What else would it be?

t_ch: 她是猎犬家系的人？
t_en: She's from the Bloodhound Family?

t_ch: 没错…恭喜你啊，世纪大迷题都被你解决了。
t_en: Bingo... congrats, you've cracked the mystery of the century.

t_ch: 她的名字叫科蒂。万万没想到…踏进匹诺康尼的第一分钟，我就立刻堕入了情网。
t_en: Her name's Coty. I never thought in a million years... the moment I stepped into Penacony, I was head over heels.

t_ch: 她的飒爽英姿瞬间击穿了我的防线。我的朋友们迫不及待地进入了梦境…而我却在苦苦思考该如何博取她的垂青。
t_en: Her heroic presence penetrated my soul. My friends were all excited about entering the dreamscape... while I was struggling to win her over.

t_ch: 如你所见，我失败了…一败涂地。她是忠诚的猎犬…而我永远当不成她眼里那只兔子。
t_en: But as you see, I failed... big time. She's a loyal Bloodhound... and I'll never be the rabbit she's after.

t_ch: 趁早死心，趁早翻篇。
t_en: Just give up already.

t_ch: 你的喜好还挺特别。
t_en: You have some unusual tastes.

t_ch: 刺耳的风凉话…但我已经习惯了，随便你吧。
t_en: Harsh sarcasm, huh...? But I'm used to it, whatever.

t_ch: 男人深情地盯着身旁的无脸拼图架，他的执着居然还透着一股可怜劲。
t_en: The guy gazes affectionately at the puzzle rack next to him, looking pitiful with his possession.

t_ch: 起码现在我已经想出了办法！要是我得不到她，那我就在这梦境里再创造出一个她。
t_en: But at least I've figured out something! If I can't have her, I'll just create another version of her in this dreamscape.

t_ch: 你——虽然你说话不中听，但我觉得你是个好人，而且是个健谈的人…和我完全不一样。你愿意帮我一次吗？
t_en: You... may not be the smoothest talker, but you seem nice and talkative... unlike me. Would you do me a favor, just this once?

t_ch: 我要在这梦境里复制出一个完美的她…一个绝对还原她真实形象的倒影。为此，我需要有人<color=#dbc291ff>帮我记录下她最真实的形象</color>……
t_en: I need to create a flawless replica of her in the dreamscape... a reflection that perfectly represents her. And for that, I need someone to <color=#dbc291ff>record her true self</color>...

t_ch: 不就是拍个照片吗？
t_en: It's just taking a photo, right?

t_ch: 对，没错…对于像你这样的人来说肯定不是什么难事吧？毕竟你既不胆小，也不拧巴……
t_en: Yeah, that's right... Shouldn't be too hard for someone like you, right? I mean, you're not a scaredy-cat or a screw-up...

t_ch: 这点事你自己不能干吗？
t_en: Can't you do it yourself?

t_ch: 我都说了，我和你不一样——很难理解吗？「胆小」、「腼腆」、「拧巴」，你难道认不得这几个词吗？
t_en: I told you we're different! Is it really that hard to understand the words "scaredy-cat" or "screw-up"?

t_ch: 答应我一件事，拍照前一定要征得她本人的同意！我不想被当成一个…呃…偏执狂。
t_en: But promise me one thing. Do ask her permission before taking the photo! I don't want to come off as... uh... pervert.

t_ch: 我要在这梦境里复制出一个完美的她…一个绝对还原她真实形象的倒影。为此，我需要有人<color=#dbc291ff>帮我记录下她最真实的形象</color>……
t_en: I need to create a flawless replica of her in the dreamscape... a reflection that perfectly represents her. And for that, I need someone to <color=#dbc291ff>record her true self</color>...

t_ch: 答应我一件事，拍照前一定要征得她本人的同意！我不想被当成一个…呃…偏执狂。
t_en: But promise me one thing. Do ask her permission before taking the photo! I don't want to come off as... uh... pervert.

t_ch: 需要帮助吗？
t_en: Do you need any help?

t_ch: 关于莫里斯……
t_en: It's about Morris...

t_ch: 莫里斯？
t_en: Morris?

t_ch: 如果您有寻人需求，可以前往前台咨询接待人员。如果有需要，猎犬家系会尽力配合……
t_en: If you need to find someone, please ask the receptionist. The Bloodhound Family is always ready to help...

t_ch: 有个叫莫里斯的客人暗恋你。
t_en: There's a visitor named Morris who has a crush on you.

t_ch: …暗恋？
t_en: ...A crush?

t_ch: 我需要一张你的照片。
t_en: I need a photo of you.

t_ch: …照片？
t_en: ...A photo?

t_ch: 抱歉，我不太能理解您的话，还请您说得再详细些。
t_en: Sorry, I didn't quite catch what you said. Could you be more specific?

t_ch: 将莫里斯的事转述给了科蒂……
t_en: You tell Coty about Morris...

t_ch: 原来如此，现在我明白了。这位客人的境遇值得同情——对此我深表遗憾。
t_en: Ah, I see, and I'm deeply sorry for that. That guest's situation deserves sympathy.

t_ch: 同情？遗憾？
t_en: Sorry? Sympathy?

t_ch: 一般人会是这个反应吗…
t_en: Is that how a normal woman would react...?

t_ch: 很明显，这位莫里斯先生不能很好地掌控自己的情感流向。想必在他的人生中曾不止一次萌生过无法得到回应的情愫。
t_en: Obviously, Mr. Morris is losing control of his emotions. He must have had plenty of unrequited feelings in his life.

t_ch: 如果凭一张照片就能缓解他的惆怅，那就把这当作家族回馈宾客的一种「服务」吧。
t_en: If my photo can make him feel better, consider it a "service" we provide to our guests.

t_ch: 我已经准备好了，您随时可以开拍。
t_en: I'm ready for my photo.

t_ch: 如果凭一张照片就能缓解莫里斯先生的惆怅，那就把这当作家族回馈宾客的一种「服务」吧。
t_en: If my photo can make Mr. Morris feel better, consider it a "service" we offer to our guests.

t_ch: 你回来了？拿到我想要的东西了吗？
t_en: You're back? Did you get what I wanted?

t_ch: 啊，科蒂……
t_en: Ah, Coty...

t_ch: 看啊，她那双眼睛…如猎犬般明亮、狡黠……
t_en: Look at her eyes... as bright and clever as a Bloodhound's...

t_ch: 这正是我需要的…这样一来，至少在这个梦境里，我可以和她成双成对……
t_en: This is exactly what I need... With this, I can finally be in her league, even if it's just in this dreamscape...

t_ch: 来吧，帮我完成这最后一片拼图吧。让我成为她的猎物…她眼中唯一的那只兔子……
t_en: Please, complete this last piece for me. Let me become the rabbit she's after... the only thing that captures attention...

t_ch: 啊，科蒂…是你！我的美梦…我的命运！
t_en: Ah, Coty... It's you! My dream... my destiny!

t_ch: 瞧瞧你，多么健全，多么完美——我愿意把我生命的*二十分之一*毫无保留地献给你……
t_en: Look at you, so flawless. I'd gladly give you *one-twentieth* of my life without thinking twice...

t_ch: 「二十分之一」？
t_en: Wait, one-twentieth?

t_ch: 当然…二十分之一难道还不够吗？
t_en: Yeah... Isn't one-twentieth more than enough?

t_ch: 人的一生注定会经历许多次的一见钟情…谁叫宇宙间充斥着如此之多的美丽之物？
t_en: Life is full of moments where you fall in love at first sight... I mean, there are so many beautiful things in the universe!

t_ch: 作为一介短生种，愿意把二十分之一的生命毫无保留地倾注给某个单独的个体…这难道还称不上忠贞和奉献吗？
t_en: I'm short-lived, yet I'm willing to dedicate one-twentieth of it to just one individual... Is that not loyalty and devotion?

t_ch: 无论如何，我得感谢你，感谢你帮我找到了这最后一块拼图。我会加倍珍惜眼前的科蒂，直到那*二十分之一*的自我彻底燃尽……
t_en: Anyway, I must thank you for getting me this last piece. I'll treasure my Coty until that *one-twentieth* of my devotion burns out...

t_ch: 科蒂…我的挚爱，我的命运！我二十分之一的美好生命……
t_en: Coty... My love, my destiny! You are one-twentieth of my amazing life...

t_ch: 男人安静地表达着他那二十分之一的浓厚爱意——复制品科蒂在他身旁不为所动。
t_en: The man pours out his heart, expressing his one-twentieth of intense love, while the replica Coty remains unfazed.

t_ch: 科蒂…我的挚爱，我的命运！
t_en: Coty... My love, my destiny!

t_ch: 为了你，我什么都愿意…哪怕得不到回应，哪怕这份感情注定要归于虚无…我也愿意为你的倒影燃烧这宝贵的二十分之一生命！
t_en: I'll do anything for you, even if I don't get a response, or even if my love is doomed... I'm ready to dedicate that precious one-twentieth of my life for your reflection!

t_ch: 在我眼里，这美梦就是为你而建立…为了这份爱，我愿意把所有与你无关的理想都深埋心底，绝不回头……
t_en: In my eyes, this dream world was made merely for you... For this love, I'm willing to bury all my irrelevant ideals deep in my heart and never look back...

t_ch: 男人安静地表达着他那二十分之一的浓厚爱意——复制品科蒂在他身旁不为所动。
t_en: The man pours out his heart, expressing his one-twentieth of intense love, while the replica Coty remains unfazed.

t_ch: 第三次的一见钟情，我爱上的是……
t_en: Well, the third time I've fallen in love at first sight...

t_ch: 男人愤慨地谈论起他第三次一见钟情的对象——复制品科蒂在他身旁不为所动。
t_en: The man resentfully recounts the experience about his third love at first sight, while the replica Coty remains unfazed.

t_ch: 我曾经爱上过一个人——她非常爱「吃」，她人生中最大的乐趣就是品尝来自各个国家、甚至各个星系的美食。
t_en: Once I fell for someone who had an insatiable love for food. She found great joy in tasting cuisine from every corner of the world, even from other galaxies.

t_ch: 为了她，我努力精进自己的厨艺…我希望让她足不出户就能品尝到来自四面八方的佳肴，这就是我展现爱的方式。
t_en: So, I dedicated myself to perfecting my culinary skills for her... I wanted her to experience the flavors of the world without leaving her home. That was my way of showing love.

t_ch: 但她是如何回报我的？她抛弃了我！践踏了我的尊严！有一天，她下班时谎称自己加班，结果却被我撞见在隔壁的快餐厅偷吃汉堡！
t_en: But how did she repay me? She abandoned me and trampled on my dignity! One day, she told me she was working overtime, but I caught her red-handed eating a burger from the fast food joint next door!

t_ch: 在我们关系的最后，她终于愿意道出实话了：她从来就不认可我的厨艺，我一直以来的行为都只是惨淡的自我感动罢了。
t_en: Towards the end of our relationship, she finally admitted the truth: She never liked my cooking, and my efforts had been nothing more than pathetic self-indulgence.

t_ch: 我愤怒！我不甘！我毅然决定离开，开始寻找我的下一个二十分之一……
t_en: I was so furious, so devastated! I decided to leave and start looking for my next one-twentieth...

t_ch: 第八次的一见钟情，我爱上的是……
t_en: Well, the eighth time I've fallen in love at first sight...

t_ch: 男人忧伤地谈论起他第八次一见钟情的对象——复制品科蒂在他身旁不为所动。
t_en: The man gloomily recounts the experience about his eighth love at first sight, while the replica Coty remains unfazed.

t_ch: 我曾经爱上过一个人——她是纯粹的享乐派，银河间最擅长玩乐的游戏女王。
t_en: I once fell for someone who was all about pure hedonism, the queen of video games.

t_ch: 为了她，我偷偷苦练以太战线，收集了我所能收集到的所有珍稀以太灵……
t_en: For her, I secretly ground hard in Aetherium Wars, collecting every rare Aether Spirit I could get my hands on...

t_ch: 我把工资的二十分之一都投入到了那款游戏上，为的只是在社区比赛上能和她同台竞技。
t_en: I even spent one-twentieth of my paycheck on the game just to be on par with her in community tournaments.

t_ch: 但她是如何回报我的？她融了我的号！擅自转走了我的限定版闪光外宇宙之火！
t_en: But how did she repay me? She stole my account and wiped out all my Aether Spirits! She even transferred my limited-edition shiny Blaze Out of Space!

t_ch: 嫉妒心过强的女人，她不能接受我在天梯总榜上居然比她的排名还高——何其狭隘！
t_en: Turns out she couldn't handle the fact that I was ranked higher than her on the leaderboard! Talk about being narrow-minded!

t_ch: 我二十分之一的工资就此打了水漂，公司无法帮我找回账号…那之后的很长一段时间，我都忘记了快乐为何物……
t_en: So, I lost one-twentieth of my paycheck, and the IPC wouldn't help me recover my account... I forgot what happiness was for a long time after that...

t_ch: 第十一次的一见钟情，我爱上的是……
t_en: Well, the eleventh time I've fallen in love at first sight...

t_ch: 男人狂热地谈论起他第十一次一见钟情的对象——复制品科蒂在他身旁不为所动。
t_en: The man enthusiastically recounts the experience about his eleventh love at first sight, while the replica Coty remains unfazed.

t_ch: 我曾经爱上过一个人——啊…那个真正带给过我快乐的人，那个差点为我的情爱生涯画上句号的人……
t_en: I once fell for someone who brought me immense joy... Someone who almost made me believe that my love life had reached its peak...

t_ch: 只是回想起来，我就禁不住想要微笑…她那么完美，和我是那么合拍…我们就像一条绿枝上结出的两朵鲜花，互相映衬，互相欣赏……
t_en: Just thinking about it brings a smile to my face. She was so perfect, so in sync with me... We were like two flowers blooming on the same branch, reflecting and appreciating each other...

t_ch: 就连与她的离别都充满了欢颜，不留任何遗憾…我和她渡过了最完美的二十分之一的人生，未来恐怕……
t_en: Even our parting was filled with joy and no regrets... I spent the most amazing one-twentieth of my life with her, and I'm afraid I won't be...

t_ch: …啊，我不是那个意思，科蒂！我向你保证，我们未来的二十分之一人生也注定与美好相随……
t_en: ...Oh, wait, Coty! That's not what I meant! I assure you, the next one-twentieth of our lives will be filled with wonderful things too...

t_ch: 你将科蒂的照片交给了莫里斯。
t_en: You handed the photo of Coty over to Morris.

t_ch: 想红就得先吃苦！
t_en: You've got to go through the grind if you want to shine!

t_ch: 现在的艺人真是娇气…
t_en: Artists these days are so delicate...

t_ch: 唉，年轻就是好啊。
t_en: Oh it's good to be young!

t_ch: 我一定会让大家成为巨星的。
t_en: I'll make sure all of them become superstars.

t_ch: 真是太过分了！{RUBY_B#「同谐」}神主{RUBY_E#}在上，愿您赐予这些艺者一点同理心吧！
t_en: This is simply too much! Seriously, please grant these artists some empathy, {RUBY_B#Harmony}O Great One{RUBY_E#}!

t_ch: 你怎么了？
t_en: What's wrong?

t_ch: 是宾客啊。多谢关心，只是工作上遇到了些烦心事——
t_en: Oh, thank you for your concern, dear guest. I've been having some trouble with my work...

t_ch: 等等，你的气质不错啊！考虑过进军娱乐圈吗？
t_en: Wait, you seem promising! Have you ever thought about getting into the entertainment industry?

t_ch: 我的梦想就是成为明星！
t_en: My dream is to become a star!

t_ch: 呵呵，那鸢尾花家系随时欢迎你，说不定下一个「盛会巨星」就是你。
t_en: Well, if that's the case, the Iris Family is always open to you. You might become the next superstar.

t_ch: 我对娱乐圈没兴趣。
t_en: I'm not interested.

t_ch: 哎呀，那还真是可惜了，难得有这么好的底子，再考虑考虑吧。
t_en: Ohh, what a shame. You've got great potential, you know? Just think about it again.

t_ch: 我已经是银河巨星了。
t_en: I'm already a cosmic superstar.

t_ch: 啊？抱歉，没想到宇宙中还有我不认识的明星。
t_en: Ugh? Sorry, I didn't realize there were stars in the universe I hadn't heard of.

t_ch: 做个自我介绍吧，我叫芙尔拉，鸢尾花家系雇佣的经纪人——准确地说，是明星经纪人。
t_en: Please allow me to introduce myself. I'm Forla, an agent working for the Iris Family. Specifically, I'm a celebrity agent.

t_ch: 我的职责是帮助家族管理与培养艺者，帮助他们成为万众瞩目的明星，以便向更多宾客宣扬「同谐」的福音。
t_en: My job is to train and manage artists for The Family, helping them shine like diamonds in the spotlight and spread the message of Harmony.

t_ch: 只可惜现在的艺者太娇气了，完全没法担起重任。刚刚就有个叫波波特的毛头小子，出道没多久就吵着要解约，说什么鸢尾花家系限制了他的发展——
t_en: But artists nowadays are just too spoiled. A kid named Popott demanded the termination of his contract with the Iris Family, whining about how we're holding him back from his full potential...

t_ch: 你听听这是什么话，太没良心了！要是没有家系，他说不定还在克劳克影城门口发传单呢！
t_en: Can you believe the audacity? If it weren't for the Iris Family, he'd still be out there handing out flyers in front of Clock Studios Theme Park!

t_ch: 算了算了，不跟你念叨这种丢自家脸面的事情了，他走了正好能把位子空出来给别人！鸢尾花需要更可靠、更懂得感恩的新鲜血液！
t_en: Ah, forget about that drama. We'll just let him leave and find someone else! The Iris Family needs fresh blood, people who are reliable and grateful!

t_ch: 我想跟你聊聊…
t_en: I'd like to chat with you...

t_ch: 行啊，我很乐意跟有潜力的年轻人交流。
t_en: Sure thing. I'm glad to chat with young people with great potential.

t_ch: 关于你的工作…
t_en: About your job...

t_ch: 你对经纪人这行感兴趣吗？这可是个苦差事，天天给娇生惯养的少爷小姐们规划前程、拉拢资源，让他们在台前受尽吹捧，这些白眼狼还不一定能领你的情。
t_en: Ever thought about becoming a manager? It's a tough business. You've got to plan the future for those spoiled young people, hustle for resources, and shower them with compliments on stage. And sometimes, those ungrateful brats don't even appreciate it!

t_ch: 他们只要听到几句宾客的夸赞就能把绒球翘上天，以为成名都是靠自己的才华，把家系的帮扶抛之脑后。
t_en: All it takes is a few compliments from the audience, and their egos shoot through the roof. They start thinking they're the sole reason for their fame, forgetting about the support they get from the Iris Family.

t_ch: 比如那个刚跑掉的波波特，试戏的时候结结巴巴的，但我看这孩子长相声音有几分可爱，就让他在《汽水人生》露了个脸。
t_en: Take that Popott, for example. He stuttered through his audition, but I saw some potential in his looks and voice, so I gave him a shot in the drama Life's a Soda.

t_ch: 你猜怎么着——这小子一炮走红！但自从他有点名气之后就开始做白日梦了，说什么自己的梦想是为宇宙带来快乐，嚷着要演那种吵吵闹闹的喜剧片。
t_en: And guess what? The kid was a hit! But as soon as he tasted fame, he started daydreaming about being in those noisy comedy pieces, thinking that was his ultimate goal.

t_ch: 一个青春偶像派的好苗子去演喜剧片，这不是在梦里做梦吗？我让他死心，维持纯真可爱的人设，今后必能成为巨星——他却说我是在绑架他的梦想，真没良心！
t_en: Can you imagine that? I told him to stick to his innocent and adorable persona and assured him he'd become a superstar soon. But he had the nerve to accuse me of crushing his dreams! Ungrateful little punk, right?

t_ch: 没良心，开除他！
t_en: Just fire that ungrateful brat!

t_ch: 呵，用不着我动手，他这不是自己就跑了吗？
t_en: Heh, I don't even have to lift a finger. He ran away all by himself.

t_ch: 我可不会随便开除手下的艺者，无论多么骄横的年轻人，只要被我选中了，我就有信心把他们调教成懂事的明星。
t_en: Look, I don't just fire artists under my wing. No matter how arrogant a youth can be, as long as they were picked by me, I'm confident I can shape them into obedient stars.

t_ch: ——而且越是看到这些年轻人如此幼稚，我就越想要改变他们。如果能让他们多为鸢尾花家系做些贡献，我受点小气算不了什么。
t_en: ...And the more I see their childish behavior, the more determined I am to change them. If I can get them to contribute more to the Iris Family, I don't mind taking some heat.

t_ch: 太憋屈了，辞职吧。
t_en: This is just too suffocating. You should quit.

t_ch: 你说笑了，我已经为鸢尾花家系干了几十年的经纪人了，哪能说走就走。
t_en: Come on, I've been working as a manager for the Iris Family for decades. I can't just up and leave.

t_ch: 如果对环境不满的从业者们轻易知难而退，何谈改变这个环境？何谈让鸢尾花家系胜过星际和平娱乐？
t_en: If all artists who are dissatisfied with the industry leave, how can we change it? And how can we Iris Family beat our competitor, Interastral Peace Entertainment?

t_ch: 年轻人不懂事，说着玩的。
t_en: Please don't mind those impulsive words from a naive youth.

t_ch: 哼，到了台前还不懂事，难道要等到曝出丑闻才懂事吗？
t_en: Naive? Can they use their naivete as an excuse when a scandal blows up in their face?

t_ch: 对明星来说，一句错话就可能毁掉职业生涯，哪有让他学着懂事的机会？我们可不像公司那样有那么多洗白明星的手段！
t_en: One wrong sentence from a star can wreck their whole career. They can't afford to be naive! We don't have those PR tricks as IPC does!

t_ch: 关于匹诺康尼的娱乐产业…
t_en: About the entertainment industry in Penacony...

t_ch: 呵呵，说到这个，我就不得不骄傲一下了——在鸢尾花家系的努力之下，匹诺康尼优秀的电影与戏剧名扬宇宙！毫无疑问，娱乐业是匹诺康尼最鲜艳的一片花瓣！
t_en: Ah, I gotta pat myself on the back for this one! Thanks to the Iris Family, Penacony's movies and dramas are renowned across the universe! The entertainment industry is undoubtedly one of Penacony's brightest jewels!

t_ch: 唉，可惜的是——这片花瓣正在不可避免地走向枯萎凋谢的命运。
t_en: But alas, this jewel is inevitably losing its luster.

t_ch: 何出此言？
t_en: Why would you say that?

t_ch: 实不相瞒，这是业内的普遍看法，因为鸢尾花家系的明星们越来越差劲了。
t_en: To be fair, it's a common view within the industry — because stars from the Iris Family are losing their edge.

t_ch: 匹诺康尼的娱乐业之所以如此繁盛，靠的就是诸多驰名星海的明星，优秀的人才资源便是鸢尾花家系最大的财富。
t_en: Penacony's entertainment industry boomed because of those stars famous across the cosmos. The outstanding talent pool is the Iris Family's greatest asset.

t_ch: 但如今投身娱乐业的逐梦客一代比一代不靠谱，公司旗下的娱乐产品也开始侵入匹诺康尼的文艺市场，前景不容乐观啊……
t_en: But nowadays, the new generation of Dreamchasers who enter the entertainment industry are getting more unreliable, and the IPC's franchises keep invading our entertainment market. The future doesn't look too bright, to be honest...

t_ch: 可能是行业环境的错。
t_en: It could be the fault of the industry's culture.

t_ch: 在下所见略同，宇宙经济的繁荣没有让行业变得更好，反而引来了不少投机倒把的家伙，天天搞些歪门邪道。
t_en: I agree. The cosmic economy boom didn't make things better in the industry. It attracted a bunch of shady speculators who engaged in all sorts of questionable practices.

t_ch: 特别是星际和平娱乐，他们肤浅的商业理念捧红了一堆德不配位的明星！没有实力，全是营销，让匹诺康尼的年轻人也受了坏影响——唉，公司！
t_en: Especially the Interastral Peace Entertainment! Their shallow business ideas have produced a bunch of unworthy and immoral stars! No talent, just marketing! And it's a bad influence on the young ones in Penacony too. IPC, what a disgrace!

t_ch: 可能是鸢尾花家系自己的问题。
t_en: It could be the Iris Family's problem.

t_ch: 这…你还真是直言不讳，不过也挺有道理的。
t_en: Well... you're the blunt type, huh? But you may have a point.

t_ch: 家系的一些高层急于扩大「同谐」在宇宙中的影响力，只想让艺者赶快走红，忽略了培养他们的价值观，搞得像是星际和平娱乐这种唯利是图的产业一样。
t_en: Some higher-ups are so obsessed with spreading the influence of Harmony throughout the universe that they only want the artists to get famous quickly, forgetting to teach them about morals. It's as if we're as much a profit-driven corporation as Interastral Peace Entertainment.

t_ch: 唉，本人身处行业之中，却无力改变现状，真是不甘心！
t_en: It's frustrating for me, working in the industry but too powerless to change the status quo!

t_ch: 可能是你的偏见。
t_en: Maybe it's just your bias.

t_ch: 切，你去看看近年希米亚影业的作品吧，演员的演技越来越差劲，实力还不如星际和平娱乐那些肤浅的偶像！
t_en: Oh, come on! Just take a look at the recent works from Himemia Films! The actors are getting worse and worse! They're not even near the superficial idols from Interastral Peace Entertainment!

t_ch: 如果作为观众想看到更优秀的艺术，就一定要抬高审美标准，宽容的态度对行业的未来有害无益！
t_en: If the audience wants to see better art, they must raise their artistic standards. Being too tolerant is killing the industry!

t_ch: 没什么想聊的了。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 可以摸摸你头上的绒球吗？
t_en: Can I touch the fluff ball on your head?

t_ch: 我的…我的绒球？哈哈，宾客，你可真是幽默啊。
t_en: My... my fluff ball? Haha, you've got a great sense of humor, dear guest.

t_ch: ——但幽默要分尺度和场合。就算你不是公众人物，也要注意言行。
t_en: ...But humor has its time and place. Even if you're not a public figure, you should watch your actions.

t_ch: 因为你永远不会知道自己什么时候会受到万众瞩目，更不会知道成名之后有哪些往事会突然浮现，让你万劫不复。
t_en: Because you never know when you might become a star. And once you are a star, you never know what shady stuff from your past might come back to haunt you.

t_ch: 想想看，当你未来意外成为宇宙中的焦点，站在人生的巅峰之时，一位皮皮西女士突然曝光了你过去的丑闻…一夜之间，亿万声赞扬变成亿万份责骂，那滋味肯定不好受吧？
t_en: Just imagine — you are at the epitome of your life, being the center of the universe's attention, and then a Pepeshi lady suddenly reveals a scandal from your past... Overnight, all the praise would turn to criticism. It won't be an easy pill to swallow.

t_ch: 这可是我在业内见过无数次的事情，你可要小心点哦。
t_en: I've seen it happen countless times in the industry. So, be careful.

t_ch: 下次再聊。
t_en: Let's catch up another time.

t_ch: 再见，祝你也能早日成名。
t_en: Farewell. May you become famous soon, too.

t_ch: 现在的艺者这点苦都吃不了，还天天想要成名，真是痴人说梦！
t_en: Those artists can't handle even the tiniest bit of stress, but they dream of fame. Can you believe that?

t_ch: 这群眼高手低的家伙！真是…真是……
t_en: What a bunch of slackers! This is really... ugh...

t_ch: 芙尔拉的怒斥声越来越小，最终变作一声哀叹，眉间的怒火被惆怅取而代之。
t_en: Forla's irritated rant gradually fades, replaced by a sigh. The anger on her face gives way to despondency.

t_ch: ——唉，真是和我当年一样啊。
t_en: ...They are just like how I used to be back in the day.

t_ch: 你以前也是明星？
t_en: You were a star, too?

t_ch: 明星？怎么可能，我只是当过一段时间的小演员而已。
t_en: A star? No way. I was just a small-time actor for a while.

t_ch: 那时我和每个刚入行的年轻人一样，相信自己终有一天会身披群星的辉光，成为宇宙中独一无二的主角。
t_en: Back then, I was like any other young person entering the industry, believing that I'd shine like a unique star in the whole universe.

t_ch: 可世上哪有那么多主角——我没有过人的天赋，没有非凡的出身，也没有绝佳的运气。付出十年血泪，最终还是一个廉价的龙套，在梦境的角落无人问津。
t_en: But there can't be that many stars out there. I had no outstanding talent, no special background, and no supreme luck. After ten years of struggles, I still ended up being a cheap extra, forgotten in the corner of the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 我的青春毁于不切实际的梦想，但比这更可怕的是——还有无数和我一样的年轻人还在重复我的悲剧。就像自灭者喜欢的飞蛾，前仆后继地飞入名为梦想的火狱。
t_en: My youth was wasted on unrealistic dreams, but there is something worse — so many other young folks like me are going down the same path of failure. They're like moths that the Self-Annihilators love so much, diving headfirst into the purgatory called "dreams."

t_ch: 所以我才会成为一名经纪人，逼着这些年轻人适应圈子里残酷的规则，让他们变成不会被梦想击垮的样子。
t_en: That's why I became a manager. I will force these kids to adapt to the cruel rules in this industry and make them indomitable before their dreams.

t_ch: 是的，是的！我想做的是鞭策他们实现自己的梦想，而不是成为阻碍他们梦想的壁垒！
t_en: That's right! I want to motivate them to chase their dreams, and never want to be a roadblock!

t_ch: 我不能让波波特就这样放弃演员的道路，他是一位很有潜力的年轻人——<color=#dbc291ff>宾客{M#先生}{F#小姐}，请您帮我把他劝回来吧！</color>
t_en: No, I can't just let Popott give up on his acting career. He's got so much potential! Please, <color=#dbc291ff>dear {M#mister}{F#miss}, could you please ask him to come back?</color>

t_ch: 没问题！
t_en: No problem!

t_ch: 你真好心，太感谢了！愿神主注视你的慷慨！
t_en: Thank you so much, kind soul! May the Great One gaze upon you for your kindness!

t_ch: 你自己去。
t_en: Go do it yourself.

t_ch: 我…对不起，我还没想清楚怎么跟他当面道歉。
t_en: I... I'm sorry... I haven't figured out how to apologize to him face-to-face.

t_ch: 但我马上就会开始准备一份符合他想法的培养方案，让他明白我的诚意，拜托你帮帮我！
t_en: But I'll start working on a training program that aligns with his ideas right away. I'll show him how sincere I am. Please, help me out!

t_ch: 波波特说自己要离开黄金的时刻——他很有可能已经在<color=#dbc291ff>黄金中央车站</color>了，麻烦你快去找找他吧，他是个<color=#dbc291ff>金发的皮皮西男孩</color>，在人群中应该很显眼！
t_en: Popott said he would leave the Golden Hour... He might be at the <color=#dbc291ff>Golden Central Station</color> by now. Please, bring him back to me. Look for a <color=#dbc291ff>Pepeshi boy with blonde hair</color>! He's standing out in the crowd!

t_ch: 喜剧片…希米亚影业…啧，这还真不是鸢尾花家系擅长的领域，怎么才能给波波特找到这种机会呢……
t_en: Comedies... Himemia Films... Tsk, that's not our strong suit. How can I get Popott an opportunity in that area...?

t_ch: 好心的宾客，请你帮我去找找波波特吧，我一定会想办法让他满意的。
t_en: Please, kind guest. Please help me find Popott. I'll make sure he's satisfied.

t_ch: 车站门口，几名气势汹汹的公司员工围着一个皮皮西男孩。
t_en: At the entrance of the station, there are a bunch of aggressive IPC employees surrounding a Pepeshi boy.

t_ch: 等、等等，我还没有想好……
t_en: H—Hold up! I haven't thought this through yet...

t_ch: 别犹豫了，波波特先生，这是对你来说是全宇宙最好的机会——你想在星际和平娱乐掌控自己的命运，还是想在家族一辈子当个傀儡？
t_en: Stop hesitating, Mr. Popott. This is the best opportunity in the whole universe for you! Do you want to control your fate at Interastral Peace Entertainment, or do you want to remain as a Family puppet for the rest of your life?

t_ch: 快走吧，别浪费部长的时间了！他的时薪可比你的演出票价高多了！
t_en: Let's go and stop wasting the leader's time! His hourly rate is much higher than the price of your show ticket!

t_ch: 先等等！
t_en: Wait!

t_ch: 没时间等了，我在四个半系统时后还有会议要开——
t_en: I can't afford to wait anymore. I have a meeting coming up in four and a half system hours...

t_ch: 不许碰他！
t_en: Don't touch him!

t_ch: 我可没碰他，请你不要侮辱我的职业素养——
t_en: I didn't lay a finger on him. Please don't question my professionalism...

t_ch: 时薪具体有多高？
t_en: How high is your hourly rate, exactly?

t_ch: 这可不能说，员工手册明令禁止对外透露薪资——
t_en: That's confidential. The employee handbook strictly prohibits the disclosure of our salary...

t_ch: 等等，你谁啊？
t_en: Wait a minute. Who are you?

t_ch: 波波特先生，这是你认识的人吗？
t_en: Mr. Popott, do you know this person?

t_ch: 不，我不认识{M#他}{F#她}啊……
t_en: Nope. I've never met {M#him}{F#her}...

t_ch: 你的经纪人让我来找你。
t_en: Your manager asked me to find you.

t_ch: 芙尔拉有话跟你说。
t_en: Forla has something to say to you

t_ch: 芙尔拉女士？哼，我跟她没什么好说的！
t_en: Madam Forla? Hmph, I've got nothing to say to her!

t_ch: 看来我司未来的艺人不是很想跟你聊天，请离开吧。
t_en: Looks like our future artist isn't too keen to talk to you. Please leave.

t_ch: 让我再跟他说几句…
t_en: Let me have a few more words with him...

t_ch: 你这家伙，听不懂部长的话吗！
t_en: What's wrong with you? Can't you understand the leader?

t_ch: 喂，别在家族的地盘丢脸！星际和平娱乐可是代表着公司的脸面！
t_en: Hey, don't make a spectacle out of yourself on The Family's turf! The Interastral Peace Entertainment represents the IPC's reputation!
Yes...

t_ch: {M#先生}{F#小姐}，请先自报家门吧，让我看看你的名头值不值得浪费我的时间。
t_en: {M#Sir}{F#Madam}, please tell me more about you. I'll see whether your title is worth my time.

t_ch: 我是星穹列车的开拓者。
t_en: I'm a Trailblazer from the Astral Express.

t_ch: 哦？这还是我第一次见到星穹列车的人。
t_en: Oh? This is the first time I've met someone from the Astral Express.

t_ch: 早就听说无名客喜欢多管闲事…但公司的繁荣离不开你们的「开拓」，所以我尊重你们的作风。
t_en: I know the Nameless are famous for meddling in other people's businesses... but as your "trailblazing" is crucial for IPC's prosperity, I respect what you do.

t_ch: 我是拯救了雅利洛-Ⅵ的英雄。
t_en: I'm the hero who saved Jarilo-VI.

t_ch: 雅利洛…哦！你难道就是帮助冰封世界结束大寒潮的无名客吗？
t_en: Jarilo... I see! Were you the Nameless who helped that icy planet to end the Eternal Freeze?

t_ch: 听说那里是筑城者们守护的世界，那你也算是为「存护」做出过贡献的朋友了。
t_en: I heard that place is protected by the Architects. In this case, you can count as a friend who had contributed to Preservation.

t_ch: 我是钟表小子的好朋友！
t_en: I'm a good friend of Clockie!

t_ch: 呃，部长，我看这人脑子好像不太好。
t_en: Um, leader, I think there's something wrong with this person's head.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，有点意思，我倒想看看鸢尾花家系为什么要找一个疯子传话。
t_en: Hahaha, this is interesting. I wonder why the Iris Family sent a nutcase here to deliver their message.

t_ch: 有什么话就快说吧——浪费的绩效算在我不讲礼貌的部下身上就好。
t_en: Spill it now. My rude subordinate here will take the rap for my subpar work efficiency today.
Huh!?

t_ch: 话没讲清楚我就不走！
t_en: I won't leave until everything is sorted!

t_ch: 啧，没想到在华贵的美梦之星，还会出现这种蛮横无理的人。
t_en: Tsk, I didn't expect there would be such brusque and unreasonable people like you on this extravagant planet of dreams.

t_ch: 保镖，让他别纠缠我和波波特先生了——注意分寸，别把家族的人引来了。
t_en: Hey, you. Stop {F#her}{M#him} from bothering me and Mr. Popott, and be careful not to turn this into a scene. I don't want to draw The Family's attention.

t_ch: 好！交给我吧！
t_en: No problem! Just leave it to me!

t_ch: 呜哇…部长，这家伙身手不一般啊！
t_en: Woah... Leader, this one here is a tough nut to crack!

t_ch: 真是没用的东西，浪费的时间从你绩效里扣！
t_en: What a useless piece of crap! I must deduct your salary to compensate for my wasted time!

t_ch: 唉，这下出差补贴全赔进去了……
t_en: Oh no, I've lost all my travel allowance...

t_ch: 至于这位{M#先生}{F#小姐}…既然你这么执着，我就让出三分钟时间吧——我倒是想看看家族要怎么把自己的员工抢回来。
t_en: {M#Sir}{F#Madam}, since you're so persistent, I'll give you three minutes to present your case. Let's see how The Family is going to get their employee back.

t_ch: 抱歉，打扰了。
t_en: Sorry. I'll get going.

t_ch: 这还差不多，算你识相！
t_en: That's more like it! You know how to read the room!

t_ch: 喂，别这么不讲礼貌——在让人迷乱的幻梦中，产生一些误会是很正常的事。
t_en: Hey, mind your manners! Misunderstandings are prone to happen in this confusing dreamland.

t_ch: 我的部下刚刚失礼了，这是星际和平娱乐的一点歉意，还请收下这些信用点…或是说苜蓿钞。
t_en: Sorry for my subordinate's rude behavior. Here are some credits... I mean, Alfalfa credits... courtesy of Interastral Peace Entertainment.

t_ch: 不愧是部长，出手真大方！
t_en: I expected no less generosity from you, leader!

t_ch: 别拍马屁了，这份赔金得从你的酬金里扣。
t_en: Spare me your flattery. I'll deduct the compensation from your pay.
Huh!?

t_ch: 别废话了，波波特，我们走吧。
t_en: Let's go, Popott. Don't waste our time on this meaningless conversation.

t_ch: …对不起，先生，要不还是让我听{M#他}{F#她}讲两句吧。
t_en: ...I'm sorry, sir. Please let me hear {M#him}{F#her} out first.

t_ch: 你…唉！你们匹诺康尼人真是麻烦，我只给三分钟，快点！
t_en: What? *sigh* You folks from Penacony are a real pain. I'll give you three minutes, so make it snappy!

t_ch: 好了，快点讲吧，芙尔拉女士到底想让你说什么？
t_en: Well, speak up now. What did Madam Forla want you to say?

t_ch: 她知道自己错了。
t_en: She admits that she's wrong.

t_ch: 她错了？哈哈哈，不是的，她没有错。
t_en: She's wrong? Hahaha, no. I don't think she is.

t_ch: 我相信她说得对，我就是更适合当个可爱的吉祥物，当个在综艺和舞台上让粉丝们欢呼尖叫的娃娃，这样更容易成为巨星——
t_en: I think she's right. I'm indeed better off being an adorable mascot who enjoys the cheers of my fans onstage during variety shows. That's an easier path to stardom for me...

t_ch: 但是她没有想过，我不想成为那样的巨星。
t_en: However, she's never thought that... maybe that's not the kind of star I want to be.

t_ch: 她给你制定了新的发展方案。
t_en: She made a new career plan for you.

t_ch: 新的发展方案…呵呵，她还是这么喜欢画大饼。
t_en: New career plan... She still likes to promise people the moon, doesn't she?

t_ch: 当初她就是这么骗我签约的，说什么让我先演那些装可爱的青春片，以后再等机会。
t_en: That's how she lured me into signing for her company back then. She told me to take the roles in those cheesy teen movies first while waiting for better opportunities to come along.

t_ch: 但我是个皮皮西人，又不是长生种，哪有那么多「以后」呢？
t_en: But I'm a Pepeshi, not a long-life species. I don't have much time to wait.

t_ch: 她都是为了你的前途。
t_en: She's doing it for your career and your own good.

t_ch: 嗯，我知道啊。
t_en: I know.

t_ch: 我从没有觉得她不在乎我的前途，相反，她很想让我成功，让我的事业一帆风顺，成为她的骄傲。
t_en: I've never felt that she doesn't care about my future. On the contrary, she really wants me to have a successful career and make her proud.

t_ch: 但她不明白，有些事对我来说比前途更重要。
t_en: But she doesn't understand that some things matter more to me than my career.

t_ch: 我之所以加入鸢尾花家系，为的就是当一个喜剧演员。我喜欢看到大家对着我笑，而不是脸红尖叫…这些话我已经跟她说过无数次了。
t_en: The sole reason that I joined the Iris Family is to be a comedian. I enjoy making people laugh, not making them blush and scream for me... I've told her this many times.

t_ch: 现在，星际和平给了我一个机会，去艾普瑟隆参演一部我真正喜欢的电影。请你告诉芙尔拉女士——我不会再受鸢尾花家系摆布了，我要把自己的人生握在自己手中！
t_en: Now, the Interastral Peace Entertainment has offered me a chance in Epsilon to act in a movie that I truly love. Please tell Madam Forla that I'll no longer subject myself to the whims of the Iris Family. I want to be the director of my own life!

t_ch: 说的好啊，自己的人生就应该把握在自己手中！
t_en: Well said! Everyone should be the director of their own life!

t_ch: 那位家族的经纪人女士真是太无情了…在我看来，只要有波波特先生这种为追逐理想不顾薪酬的意志，就一定能获得成功！这份逐梦客的精神正是我看中他的原因！
t_en: That manager from The Family is so unkind... I believe Mr. Popott will make it big with his determination to achieve his dream, regardless of compensation! His dreamchasing spirit is why I picked him!

t_ch: 你真是个好人。
t_en: You're such a good person.

t_ch: 多谢夸奖，尊重人才正是我们星际和平公司的总要准则。
t_en: Thanks for your compliment. Respecting talents is our fundamental value at the IPC.

t_ch: 我怎么没感觉到，每天不是罚工资就是扣绩效……
t_en: Are you sure? How come I'm getting either a pay cut or a KPI deduction every other day...?

t_ch: 你真这么觉得？
t_en: You really think that?

t_ch: 当然！波波特先生只是长得可爱了一点，可爱就不能当喜剧明星了吗？
t_en: Of course! Mr. Popott looks a tad cute, but who said you can't be a comedian when you're cute?

t_ch: 我看那个芙尔拉女士就是把人当作工具，只看重员工的商业价值，却不在乎员工的精神追求，真是令人痛心！
t_en: If you ask me, I'd say that Madam Forla is only treating her talents as tools. She only cares about how much she can earn from her employees, instead of listening to what her employees really want. That's saddening!

t_ch: 什么时候也能在乎一下我的精神追求，我是为了当武打演员才入职的……
t_en: Can somebody listen to what I really want, too? I joined this company to be a martial art actor...

t_ch: 重点是「不顾薪酬」吧…
t_en: The key is "regardless of compensation," no?

t_ch: 这、这是波波特先生决心和毅力的体现啊！就算他这么说了，难道堂堂星际和平娱乐，还能亏待他不成？
t_en: Th—This shows Mr. Popott's determination and perseverance! Even if he says so, can the esteemed Interastral Peace Entertainment really mistreat him?

t_ch: 这还真不好说……
t_en: That's hard to say...

t_ch: <size=28>咳咳！</size>
t_en: <size=28>Ahem!</size>

t_ch: 好了，波波特先生，你现在决定跟我走了吗？
t_en: Mr. Popott, are you ready to come with me?

t_ch: 嗯，我想明白了，今后请多多指教！
t_en: Yes, I've thought it all through. I'm ready to get on board!

t_ch: 你看，这位皮皮西已经做出了选择，一个明智的选择。别遗憾，我会给这个年轻人更好的未来。
t_en: Look, this Pepeshi has made his decision — and what a brilliant decision. Don't worry, I'll make sure he has a bright future ahead of him.

t_ch: 反正要离开匹诺康尼了，这些东西就作为同行挖角的赔礼送给你吧——下次别再把这么有潜力的小明星放跑了。
t_en: Since we're leaving Penacony, you can keep these as compensation for poaching your talent. Don't let an artist with such great potential go next time.

t_ch: 衣冠楚楚的男人掏出了一沓苜蓿币，递到了你的手中。
t_en: The well-dressed man takes out a wad of Alfalfa credits and presses it into your hands.

t_ch: 波、波波特！对不起，我刚刚说的太过分了，你看看这份新的发展规划……
t_en: P—Popott! I'm sorry for my harsh remark earlier. Please take a look at this new proposal...

t_ch: …诶，宾客，波波特呢？
t_en: ...Eh? Dear guest, where's Popott?

t_ch: 他已经走了。
t_en: He's gone.

t_ch: 这样呀…也没办法，毕竟是我自己把他逼走的。
t_en: I see... Well, it was me who drove him away.

t_ch: 对不起，我失败了…
t_en: I'm sorry for failing to make him stay...

t_ch: 这样呀…没事，你愿意帮我这个忙，我就已经很感谢了。
t_en: There's no need to feel bad... It's kind enough of you to offer your help.

t_ch: 没关系！
t_en: It's all right!

t_ch: 你…呵呵，我明白了，看来我的赔礼白准备了。
t_en: You... Fine, I get it. Seems like my effort to make amends has gone down the drain.

t_ch: 唉，波波特，如果我早点明白你的想法就好了。是我的傲慢葬送了一个年轻人的未来，让宇宙中少了一位本应流芳百世的巨星。
t_en: Alas, Popott, if only I could understand you earlier. My arrogance has deprived a young man of a bright future and the universe of a future superstar.

t_ch: 犯下了这种罪过，我已经没脸自称什么明星经纪人了。或许，我确实该放弃这份工作了……
t_en: I've messed up too badly to continue calling myself a celebrity agent. Perhaps I really should give up on this career...

t_ch: 他还会继续当演员的。
t_en: He'll still be an actor.

t_ch: 他只是去星际和平娱乐了。
t_en: He's just joined Interastral Peace Entertainment.

t_ch: 真、真的吗？那真是太好了！
t_en: R—Really? That's great!

t_ch: 只要他还能继续追逐梦想就好，如果真的因为我的错误就让一个孩子放弃梦想，我的绒球得永远装满愧疚了。
t_en: I'm happy as long as he keeps pursuing his dream. If he gave up because of me, my fluff ball would be filled with guilt forever.

t_ch: 我没办法亲自帮他实现那个梦想了，真是可惜，我大概会遗憾一辈子吧。
t_en: It's a shame that I can no longer help him in his pursuit. I'll probably regret it for the rest of my life.

t_ch: ——不过我手下还有很多艺者：有相貌平平，但想要成为模特的演员；有仪表端庄，但想要追求个性的偶像；有音乐风格小众，却想红遍宇宙的歌手……
t_en: However, there are still many other artists under my management. Some aspire to become models despite having average looks, some are good-looking but dream of becoming idols with an unconventional style, while some compose niche music but yearn to be worldwide popular singers...

t_ch: 从今天起，我绝不会再成为浇灭别人梦想的冷水了！*皮皮西族粗口*！去他的市场受众，去他的家族利益，去他的宇宙巨星！我会成为点亮他们梦想的柴薪，为过去的傲慢赎罪！
t_en: From now on, I'll stop dampening other people's dreams! *Pepeshi vulgar word*! I'll forget about the target audience, The Family's benefits, or making universal superstars! Instead, I'll become the firewood that nurtures the first sparks of their dreams to atone for my past arrogance!

t_ch: 可恶！要不是现在的市场审美如此狭隘，我和艺者们何必如此痛苦！
t_en: My talents and I wouldn't suffer so much if the current market didn't have such a narrow taste when it comes to art!

t_ch: 对！都怪这些消费者不能接受更多元的艺术——要想帮助艺者实现梦想，就必须要先改变他们的审美！
t_en: Exactly! It's the fault of those consumers who aren't receptive to more diverse art! If I want to help my talents realize their dreams, I must convince the consumers to change their preferences first!

t_ch: 你看到芙尔拉皱起了眉头，用她的小短腿狠狠跺脚，就像你刚见到她的时候一样暴躁。
t_en: You see Forla stomping furiously with a frown on her forehead. She is as grumpy as the time you first met her.

t_ch: 可恶！要不是现在的市场审美如此狭隘，我和艺者们何必如此痛苦！
t_en: My talents and I wouldn't suffer so much if the current market didn't have such a narrow taste when it comes to art!

t_ch: 对！都怪这些消费者不能接受更多元的艺术——要想帮助艺者实现梦想，就必须要先改变他们的审美！
t_en: Exactly! It's the fault of those consumers who aren't receptive to more diverse art! If I want to help my talents realize their dreams, I must convince the consumers to change their preferences first!

t_ch: 大家的审美也能被改变？
t_en: Is it possible to change the public's preferences and tastes?

t_ch: 宾客，「纯美」的星神早就不见了——所以「美」的概念可以被随便定义。只要计划够精密、手段够巧妙，昨天丑陋的事物就会在明天变成绝美的艺术！
t_en: Dear guest, the Aeon of Beauty is long gone, which means everyone can define "beauty" however they like. With a well-thought-out plan and clever means, you can turn what was deemed ugly yesterday into a masterpiece tomorrow!

t_ch: 让我给你说个我听说过的例子吧：两个琥珀纪前，星际和平娱乐举行了一场歌手比赛，希望能挖掘宇宙中的音乐人才。
t_en: Let me give you an example I've heard of: Two Amber Eras ago, the Interastral Peace Entertainment hosted a singing competition, hoping to discover musical talents across the universe.

t_ch: 在这场比赛上，出现了一群穿着简陋、行为粗野的星际流浪汉，他们来自某个被毁灭的原始星球，唱着难听无比的歌声，像是忆域迷因的鬼叫。
t_en: A group of shabbily dressed and boorish interstellar wanderers showed up from a destroyed primitive planet. Their singing voice was so terrible that it sounded like the haunting cries from the Memory Zone Meme.

t_ch: 但星际和平娱乐当时的营销部长却突发奇想，给了他们做了一个包装——说他们是某个已逝星神的传人，唱的是失传的艺术瑰宝。
t_en: However, the marketing director of Interastral Peace Entertainment at the time had a whimsical idea. He packaged the wanderers as the descendants of a deceased Aeon and claimed that they were singing an artistic piece that was long lost.

t_ch: 你猜怎么着？蠢蛋观众全都信了这份鬼话，开始把这帮流浪汉当成艺术家追捧！
t_en: Guess what? The idiotic audience all bought the story and started idolizing these wanderers as artists!

t_ch: 呼——所以无论艺者们想做多么标新立异的事，只要我的商业方案够精妙，都能把他们包装成明星！
t_en: Hmm... Therefore, no matter how unconventional our artists want to be, we can turn them into stars as long as we have a brilliant enough business plan!

t_ch: 虽说我要让这些年轻人实现梦想，但不知道他们能否背负起梦想的重量。
t_en: As much as I want to help these youngsters achieve their dreams, I'm not sure whether they're ready to pay the price.

t_ch: 希望他们能做好心理准备——选择一条崎岖的路，就要面临更多的挫折与苦难。
t_en: I hope they have mentally prepared themselves. They're bound to face more setbacks and hardship when they choose a path less traveled.

t_ch: 芙尔拉低下了头，露出凝重的神情，这份严肃在皮皮西人稚嫩的面孔上反倒有些滑稽。
t_en: Forla lowers her head with a solemn expression, which looks somewhat comical on a Pepeshi's youthful face.

t_ch: 虽说我要让这些年轻人实现梦想，但不知道他们能否背负起梦想的重量。
t_en: As much as I want to help these youngsters achieve their dreams, I'm not sure whether they're ready to pay the price.

t_ch: 他们有人想要传承过去的艺术，有人想要尝试更前卫的表达…但无论如何，他们的作品还是要摆在观众面前，供市场评判。
t_en: Some of them want to carry on traditional arts, while some are experimenting with a more avant-garde performance style... Anyhow, their work has to be presented to the public and evaluated by the market.

t_ch: 我曾在江户星乐寮中聆听古老曼丽的歌谣，在星泪贡多拉上感受哀切悲痛的泣歌。也曾在朋克洛德体验令人目眩的光感艺术，在迷宫之星观看奇诡怪异的谜语影片。
t_en: I once listened to beautiful ancient ballads at Edo Star's houses of music and immersed myself in the sorrowful tunes on the Startear Gondola. I admired the dazzling light art at Punklorde and watched mysterious and bizarre puzzle videos on the Maze Planet.

t_ch: 它们有的是宇宙中古老的艺术遗产，有的是银河中前卫的探索表达…但这些事物都无法让市场认可，难以收获追捧，还会面临非议与偏见。
t_en: Some of them are ancient artistic heritage lost in the universe, while others are avant-garde expressions on a cosmic scale... but none of them are recognized by the market. Not only do they get zero attention from the public, but they also face criticism and prejudice.

t_ch: 希望他们能做好心理准备——选择一条崎岖的路，就要面临更多的挫折与苦难。
t_en: I hope they have mentally prepared themselves. They're bound to face more setbacks and hardship when they choose a path less traveled.

t_ch: 哈哈哈…我猜这些年轻人听到我的想法一定会很开心的！
t_en: Hahaha... I can imagine how thrilled those youngsters would be when they find out what I think!

t_ch: 我会把之前给他们制定的方向全都丢掉，然后告诉他们——去成为你们所憧憬的样子吧，我会帮助你们的！
t_en: I'll set aside all the plans I've previously made for them. I'll encourage them to be whatever they want to be and reassure them that they'll have my help!

t_ch: 芙尔拉放下了自己端着的架子，开怀大笑起来，像个天真可爱的孩子。
t_en: Forla lets go of her pretensions and laughs heartily like a carefree kid.

t_ch: 哈哈哈…我猜这些年轻人听到我的想法一定会很开心的！
t_en: Hahaha... I can imagine how thrilled those youngsters would be when they find out what I think!

t_ch: 我会把之前给他们制定的方向全都丢掉，然后告诉他们——去成为你们所憧憬的样子吧，我会帮助你们的！
t_en: I'll set aside all the plans I've previously made for them. I'll encourage them to be whatever they want to be and reassure them that they'll have my help!

t_ch: 等他们得到了自由，就能充满干劲地去追逐梦想…也许还能开辟鸢尾花从来没有发现过的娱乐业蓝海——我说不定也能沾点光，在家族里混个一官半职！
t_en: Once they have freedom, they'll be able to chase their dreams with enthusiasm... They might even stumble upon a blue ocean of the entertainment industry for the Iris Family! I might even benefit a little from it and secure a nice position in The Family because of them!

t_ch: 到时候，我就有权力去改变鸢尾花家系了…然后我会从匹诺康尼开始，慢慢改变娱乐行业的状况，让所有人的梦想都有展示的舞台……我会成为偶像们的偶像，被宇宙铭记……
t_en: When the time comes, I'll have the authority to bring changes to the Iris Family... Then, starting from Penacony, I'll slowly change the entertainment industry and provide a platform for everyone to realize their dreams... I'll be idolized by idols and etch my name in the history of the universe...

t_ch: 芙尔拉自说自话地描绘着未来的图景，忘记了你的存在。
t_en: Forla gets carried away while talking to herself and envisioning her future, becoming oblivious to your presence.

t_ch: 原来这一切在他眼里都不重要…我，做出的所有努力，都变成了他逃离我的原因。
t_en: It turns out that none of these things actually mattered to him... Everything I've done became the reason he ran away from me.

t_ch: 或许比起管理能力和市场知识，我更该学习的是如何了解艺者们的心吧……
t_en: Perhaps I should learn how to understand the hearts of my talents instead of management skills and market knowledge...

t_ch: 芙尔拉微微颤抖，你看到她的眼角闪烁泪光——她完全抛下了自己的面子，哇哇大哭起来。
t_en: As Forla trembles slightly, you notice tears glistening in her eyes. She then bursts into tears and lets go of all her pretences.

t_ch: 呜、呜呜呜！波波特，这个傻孩子，就不能再给我一次机会嘛——
t_en: Waaaa! Popott, you foolish boy! Can't you give me another chance...

t_ch: 那么多艺者里，我对同为皮皮西人的他最用心，给了他最丰厚的薪酬，最优越的资源，最高的期望……
t_en: Of all the talents, I invested the most effort in him because he's a fellow Pepeshi. I gave him the most generous pay and the best resources, and I had the highest expectations for him...

t_ch: 我现在才知道，原来这一切在他眼里都不重要…我，做出的所有努力，都变成了他逃离我的原因。
t_en: Only now do I realize that none of those things mattered to him... Everything I've done became the reason he ran away from me.

t_ch: 或许比起管理能力和市场知识，我更该学习的是如何了解艺者们的心吧……
t_en: Perhaps I should learn how to understand the hearts of my talents instead of management skills and market knowledge...

t_ch: 拉洛丝，你来了！我有个好消息要告诉你！
t_en: You're here, Larose! I have good news for you!

t_ch: 芙尔拉女士，我也有话要对您说……
t_en: Madam Forla, I have something to tell you too...

t_ch: 你先听我这个好消息吧——在之后的演唱会上，你可以献唱真正的「纯美之歌」了！
t_en: Why don't you hear me out first? Listen, you can perform the real "Song of Beauty" at your future concerts!

t_ch: 我已经想通了，咱们把那些骗人的歌忘了吧，我要让你成为宇宙中独一无二的音乐艺术家，让你能自由地唱出自己喜欢的歌曲！
t_en: I've thought it all through! Let's forget about all those bogus songs! I'm going to make you the most unique musician in the universe, and you'll be free to sing the songs you like!

t_ch: 拉洛丝，怎么了？你难道不想唱你最喜欢的歌曲吗？
t_en: What's the matter, Larose? Don't you want to perform the songs you like?

t_ch: 对，我不想唱。
t_en: Yes. I don't want to.

t_ch: 什么？我没听错吧！？
t_en: What? Did I hear you wrong!?

t_ch: 芙尔拉女士，我现在正在事业上升期，应该抓住势头，走上更大的舞台——这是您告诉我的，现在突然让我唱这种音乐，会引起观众的反感吧。
t_en: Madam Forla, my career is heading upward now. I should seize the momentum to get on bigger platforms — that's what you taught me. If I start to sing this kind of music now... It won't sit well with the audience.

t_ch: 这…拉洛丝，你怎么突然害怕起这种事情了。你之前想唱「纯美之歌」的时候，不是说自己不怕争议吗？为了实现梦想，你可不能这么胆小啊……
t_en: But... Larose, since when did you start to let those things hold you back? Didn't you say you weren't worried about controversies when you asked to perform the "Song of Beauty"? You can't be so timid if you want to realize your dream...

t_ch: 芙尔拉女士，就是您让我变成这样的！在我想要自由歌唱的时候，您说我是痴心妄想。如今我选择脚踏实地，您却又说我胆小，让我大胆追逐理想。
t_en: Madam Forla, it was you who made me this way! Back when I yearned to sing freely, you said I was being unrealistic. Now that I've chosen to become more grounded, you're telling me I'm too timid and should instead chase my dream bravely.

t_ch: 我是人，不是只会听从您命令的自动乐团，请不要再这样折磨我了。我也不会让「纯美之歌」被世人肆意诋毁，这是对「纯美」女神的不敬。
t_en: I'm human, not an automated orchestra that only obeys your commands. Please stop torturing me this way. I won't allow the "Song of Beauty" to be disparaged by the world, as it would be disrespectful to the Goddess of Beauty.

t_ch: 今后我会好好听您的话，不会再想这些幼稚的事情了——也请您努力让拉洛丝成为一名「盛会巨星」，而不是那种孤芳自赏的小艺术家。
t_en: From today onward, I'll follow your arrangements and stop dwelling on those childish thoughts. So, please do your part and make me a superstar, not a small-time artist whose music is admired by no one.

t_ch: 我、我知道了，但是，我们要不要再聊聊你的未来……
t_en: I—I've got it. Still, should we spend more time discussing your future...

t_ch: 抱歉，我得去继续宣传演唱会了，这可是您给我的工作，再见——
t_en: Sorry, I've got to attend a promotional activity for my concert — a job you scheduled for me. See you...

t_ch: 拉洛丝…如果我能早点做出改变，你是不是就不会放弃原本的理想了呢……
t_en: Larose... Would you have continued chasing your dream if I had come around earlier?

t_ch: 这也没什么不好的。
t_en: It isn't necessarily a bad thing.

t_ch: …确实，凭她的资质，只要延续之前的风格演出下去，一定就能获得成功——但这真的是她想要的吗？
t_en: ...Indeed, with her talent, I'm sure she'll rise to the top as long as she carries on with her current performance style. Yet, is it really what she desires?

t_ch: 她现在有了新的理想。
t_en: She has a new dream now.

t_ch: …是啊，她说自己想成为「盛会巨星」——如果这真的是她现在想要的东西，我也会尽力帮她得到。
t_en: ...Yes, she said she wanted to become a superstar... If that's truly what she wants, I'll do my best to help her.

t_ch: 唉，想法可以轻易转变，但对艺者们造成的影响没法轻易消除…看来我的过错不能洗刷，只能慢慢补赎。
t_en: Alas, it's easy to change my mindset, but the impact of my actions on my talents is hard to erase... Looks like my mistakes can't be erased overnight, and must be slowly made up for my mistake.

t_ch: 宾客，请你之后也多支持一下拉洛丝吧，希望你能给她的梦想一份力量…祝你有一天也能实现自己的梦想！
t_en: Dear guest, please show your support to Larose in the future and give her the strength to pursue her dream. I wish you could realize yours one day too!

t_ch: （大家似乎对拉洛丝的音乐不太满意……）
t_en: (People don't seem impressed by Larose's music...)

t_ch: 亲爱的{M#先生}{F#小姐}，您准备好欣赏声乐之星的「纯美之歌」了吗？
t_en: Are you ready for the Song of Beauty from the planet of canticles, dear {M#sir}{F#lady}?

t_ch: 声乐之星？
t_en: The planet of canticles?

t_ch: 纯美之歌？
t_en: The Song of Beauty?

t_ch: 啊，看来您并不了解我的音乐呀。让我来介绍一下吧——不知您是否听说过「门达希亚」这个名字？
t_en: I see, you are not familiar with my music. Let me introduce it to you: Have you ever heard of the name "Mendasia"?

t_ch: 是声乐之星的名字吗？
t_en: Is that the name of the planet of canticles?

t_ch: 是的，它曾经是一个宇宙中的艺术天堂，但如今已不复存在。
t_en: Yes, it was once an artistic paradise in the universe, but it no longer exists.

t_ch: 是一个人名吗？
t_en: Is it someone's name?

t_ch: 人名…如果把它比喻为一个人，那一定是位仙姿玉貌的女神，但它其实是声乐之星的名字。
t_en: Someone's name... Well, if you compare it to "someone," then she must be a goddess of immortal beauty. It's actually the name of the planet of canticles.

t_ch: 是吃的吗？
t_en: Is it some sort of food?

t_ch: 吃的…呵呵，这其实是声乐之星的名字，不过您的玩笑道出了它最后的命运。
t_en: Some sort of food... Hehe, it's actually the name of the planet of canticles, but your joke relates to its ultimate fate.

t_ch: 门达希亚自文明诞生以来便信奉「纯美」，伊德莉拉女神赠予了我们隔绝灾厄的镜壁，这个世界便隐藏了自己在星海中的痕迹，因此鲜有人知晓它的存在。
t_en: Since the dawn of civilization, Mendasia has believed in the Beauty. Idrila bestowed upon us the Wall of Mirrors that shielded us from plagues. Henceforth, the world concealed its traces in the star ocean, and only a few knew of its existence.

t_ch: 那里逐渐变为了一个安宁祥和的仙境，人们在花林中饭蔬饮水，潜心探究用音律赞颂纯美，终日弦歌不绝，自诩「声乐之星」。
t_en: Gradually, it became a peaceful wonderland where people indulged in feasts and drinks in the forest of flowers, explored and sang in praise of the Beauty, referring to their homeland as the planet of canticles.

t_ch: 但女神退隐世间后，祂留下的赐福也逐渐碎裂…就在两个琥珀纪前，镜壁彻底坍塌。门达希亚如同刚羽化的蝴蝶卷入风暴，不久便被一只可怕的星际巨兽吞噬。
t_en: However, as the Beauty disappeared, the blessings she left behind gradually crumbled... Approximately two Amber Eras ago, the Wall of Mirrors collapsed entirely, and soon after, like a vulnerable butterfly, Mendasia was swallowed by a terrifying interstellar beast.

t_ch: 幸存的门达希亚人自此开始流浪，无人相信星际难民口中的世外桃源，声乐之星存在过的证据只有几首叫做「纯美之歌」的音乐……
t_en: The surviving Mendasians have been wandering ever since, and no one believes in the paradise spoken of by those refugees. Only a few musical pieces, known as the "Song of Beauty," serve as evidence of the existence of the planet of canticles...

t_ch: 纯美的仙境已逝，如果它残存的艺术也随之凋亡，宇宙艺术的冠冕上定会失去一颗耀眼的明珠。每多一位听众，它存续的火光便会亮上一分——所以希望您能支持我的演出。
t_en: The enchanting realm of the Beauty has faded, and if its remaining art withers away, the crown of cosmic art will lose one of its brightest jewels. With each additional listener, the enduring flame of art will shine brighter. So, I hope you can support my performance.

t_ch: 我一定会支持的！
t_en: I'll definitely support you!

t_ch: 谢谢您的支持，代表纯美女神向您致敬。
t_en: Thank you for your support. I salute you on behalf of the Beauty.

t_ch: 我对音乐演出不感兴趣。
t_en: I'm not interested in musical performances.

t_ch: 是吗…那太遗憾了，这可是纯美女神遗留在宇宙中为数不多的遗产。
t_en: Oh, what a shame... It's one of the few legacies that the Beauty has left behind in the universe.

t_ch: 我会继续努力传唱「纯美之歌」，为了声乐之星，也为了传承宇宙中珍贵的美好。
t_en: I will continue to preserve the "Song of Beauty," for the sake of the planet of canticles and the precious beauty of the universe.

t_ch: 为了让「纯美之歌」继续在银河中传唱，还请您多多支持我的演出。
t_en: Please support my performances so that the "Song of Beauty" will continue resonating in the cosmos.

t_ch: 我想跟你聊聊…
t_en: I'd like to chat with you...

t_ch: 好啊，我也很想让听众多了解我与我的音乐。
t_en: Sure thing. I am delighted to provide my audience with more information about myself and my music.

t_ch: 你为什么在街头发传单？
t_en: Why are you handing out flyers on the streets?

t_ch: 其实我的演出以前在匹诺康尼一直很受欢迎，不过最近音乐会的门票销量很不理想，所以经纪人让我来街头亲自推广演出。
t_en: Actually, my concerts used to be quite popular in Penacony, but ticket sales have been poor lately, so my manager suggested that I personally promote the show on the streets.

t_ch: 我想是因为「谐乐大典」快开始了，所有的宾客都在期盼知更鸟前辈登台，对其他演出却不屑一顾……
t_en: I believe it's because the Charmony Festival is approaching, and all the visitors are eager for Robin's performance, but not so much for the other acts...

t_ch: 梦境中追逐音乐梦想的人多如繁星，听众却只凝望最耀眼的明月…一直这样下去，微小的辰星就只能攀附月光，或在孤独落寞中走向黯淡，艺术的星空迟早会变得单调无味……
t_en: Countless individuals are pursuing their musical aspirations in the dreamscape, yet the audience fixates only on the brightest moon... If this trend continues, the lesser stars will only shine clinging to the moonlight or fade away in solitude. Sooner or later, the sky will become insipid...

t_ch: 亲爱的{M#先生}{F#小姐}，也请您在游览之余多关注一下其他演出吧。虽然不能与谐乐大典的隆盛相比，但肯定也有自己独特的魅力。
t_en: Dear {M#sir}{F#lady}, while you are here, please pay attention to some other performances. Although they may not match the grandeur of the Charmony Festival, each has its own unique charm.

t_ch: 我只想听最好的演出
t_en: I just want to enjoy the finest shows.

t_ch: 我理解您的追求，但每个人应该都对艺术都有不一样的评判标准。如果您只相信外界的评价，可能反而会错失「最好的演出」。
t_en: I understand your desire, but everyone has their own criteria for the "finest" shows. By relying solely on external judgments, you might overlook the truly finest shows.

t_ch: 您可以试着从我的演出开始，接触一些不一样的音乐形式。或许…「纯美之歌」在您耳中不会逊色于知更鸟前辈的歌声。
t_en: Perhaps you could start with my performance and expose yourself to different musical forms. Who knows... the Song of Beauty might resonate with you as much as Robin's songs.

t_ch: 有什么能推荐的演出吗？
t_en: Are there any performances that you would recommend?

t_ch: 那可太多了，绿洲的时刻中，自动乐团的演奏永不落幕；蓝调的时刻中，漫游乐手布罗娜举办了游轮演出；太阳的时刻中，巨星爱卡琳主演的歌剧《梦之王》正在辉煌剧场热演……
t_en: There are numerous options... the automated orchestra at the Moment of Oasis, the wandering musician Brona's cruise show during the Blue Hour, and Ekarin's famous opera, the King of Dreams, at the Moment of Sol...

t_ch: 而在黄金的时刻，一位来自「声乐之星」的遗民也将献唱她家乡的歌谣——请{M#先生}{F#小姐}不要忘记我的音乐会哦。
t_en: And in the Golden Hour, a talented songstress from the planet of canticles will be performing songs from her lost hometown. Please, don't forget about my concert. Please, dear {M#sir}{F#lady}, don't forget about my concert!

t_ch: 你是怎么成为匹诺康尼的歌手的？
t_en: How did you become a singer in Penacony?

t_ch: 打我记事起，我就随长辈一起四处卖唱。从边星小国到佣兵船舰，我们走到哪里就唱到哪里，演出的报酬只能勉强满足生存。
t_en: Well, for as long as I can remember, I have been traveling and singing with my relatives. We performed in various places, from small countries on border stars to mercenary ships, but the pay we received was barely enough to make ends meet.

t_ch: 有次我在萨兰陨石带的一颗彗星酒馆中驻唱，一位皮皮西女士突然打断了我的表演，说我的歌喉不应该浪费在这种地方，应该走向更大的舞台。
t_en: Once, while I was singing at The Comet tavern in the Saran Meteorite Belt, a Pepeshi lady interrupted my performance, saying that my talent should not be wasted in a place like that, and that I should move on to a bigger stage.

t_ch: 我本以为她只是位醉酒的客人，但后来我才知道，她是鸢尾花家系最著名的明星经纪人——芙尔拉女士。我接受了她的邀请来到匹诺康尼，成了梦境中小有名气的歌星。
t_en: At first, I thought she was just some random drunken customer, but I later discovered that she was Forla, the most respected celebrity manager from the Iris Family. So I accepted her invitation to Penacony and became a songstress with a smidgen of fame in the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 她真的是一位非常严格的经纪人，我的饮食起居、日程安排、人际关系以及演出内容都必须听从她的安排，像这样能和您闲聊的机会其实非常难得……
t_en: She's a very strict manager who oversees my diet, schedule, relationships, and performance. It's rare for me to have a chance to chat with you like this...

t_ch: 如何评价知更鸟？
t_en: What are your thoughts on Robin?

t_ch: 知更鸟前辈？「百年一遇的音乐天才」、「希佩亲吻过她的歌喉」、「谐乐的公主」…您肯定也听过这些盛名，应该不用我多加赘述了。
t_en: Robin? You mean the "once-in-a-century musical genius," the "voice kissed by Xipe themselves," and the "Princess of Harmony?" I don't think I need to elaborate on her reputation.

t_ch: 她的作品确实非常优秀，不过…自从我来到匹诺康尼后，就发现每面墙壁都张贴着她的海报、每个店面都在播放她的音乐、每个擦肩而过的路人都在哼唱她的歌曲……
t_en: Her songs are undoubtedly excellent, but since I arrived in Penacony, I noticed her posters adorning every wall, her music playing in every store, and her songs hummed by everyone passing by...

t_ch: 打个比方——苏乐达是公认的美味，但如果您每天的饮品都只有苏乐达，甚至连洗漱用水都变成了苏乐达，日复一日、年复一年…如此一来，您还会喜爱它吗？
t_en: For example, SoulGlad is widely recognized as a delicacy, but if you had to drink SoulGlad every single day, even use it for washing... day after day, year after year... would you still love it in the same way?

t_ch: 想想就反胃…
t_en: That's a nauseating thought...

t_ch: 是啊。无论是多么美好的事物，它的魅力都会在一次次的重复体验中流失。
t_en: Yeah, no matter how good something is, its charm diminishes with repeated exposure.

t_ch: 当然会，我不会背叛美味。
t_en: Of course I'd love it. I'll never betray good flavor.

t_ch: 呃…怪我例子举得不恰当。您还真喜欢苏乐达，怪不得会来到黄金的时刻。
t_en: Um... looks like I cited an inappropriate example. Now I see you truly enjoy SoulGlad. No wonder you are here, at the Golden Hour.

t_ch: 我很敬重她，但匹诺康尼的音乐不应该一直活在她的阴影下，梦境应该拥抱更多样的音乐风格。
t_en: I have great respect for her, but the music in Penacony should not be overshadowed by her all the time. Dreamscape should embrace a more diverse range of musical styles.

t_ch: 下次再聊。
t_en: Let's catch up another time.

t_ch: 嗯，我随时愿意跟宾客交流。
t_en: Sure, I'm always up for chatting with guests.

t_ch: 回头见。
t_en: See you later.

t_ch: 回头见，期待能在观众席上看到您的身影。
t_en: See you later. I look forward to seeing you in the audience.

t_ch: 拉洛丝恬静的面孔忽然洋溢出欢快的神色，她启口而歌，用你从未听过的声调唱出无法理解的语言——
t_en: All of a sudden, Larose's serene face lights up with joy as she opens her mouth and sings in an unfamiliar language, in a tone you have never heard before.

t_ch: 「Mendaxia~Mendaxia~tuven arztis liberan sanam~nururisha Idrila bfularun……」
t_en: "Mendasia~ Mendasia~ Tuven arztis liberan sanam~ Nururisha Idrila bfularun..."

t_ch: 歌曲的每个音节都像是在模仿自然的声音，树叶摩挲、虫鸟啼鸣、野兽低吼、雨水坠地…杂乱无章的声音组成了一种奇妙的韵律。
t_en: Each syllable of the song seems to imitate the sounds of nature: Rustling of leaves, buzzing of insects, chirping of birds, low growls of beasts, raindrops hitting the ground... The amalgamation of sounds creates a marvelous rhythm.

t_ch: 歌声时而尖锐，时而粗野，你甚至不知道灵长目的声带能发出这样的声响，也无法评价这样的音乐是否能称得上动听，但你能感受到歌声中狂野的活力。
t_en: The song is sometimes shrill, sometimes raw, and you didn't even know these are sounds producible by a human's vocal cords. It's hard to tell if the music can be called "melodious," but the wild vigor within the song is palpable.

t_ch: 呼——这是我小时候学会的第一首歌，不知道为什么就唱出口了…真是奇怪。
t_en: Phew... That was the first song I learned as a child, and I don't know why I sang it out loud... How peculiar.

t_ch: 你准备拿这首歌来演出？
t_en: Will you perform this song at your concert?

t_ch: 不，如果您真的光临了我的音乐会，其实是听不到这首歌的。
t_en: No, if you attend my concert, you won't actually hear this song.

t_ch: 这就是「纯美之歌」？
t_en: Is this the Song of Beauty?

t_ch: 是的，这是门达希亚最具有代表性的民谣，但其实我从未在匹诺康尼唱响过它。
t_en: Yes, it's the most iconic ballad of Mendasia, but I have never performed it in Penacony.

t_ch: 这首歌的语言已经无人使用，但每句歌词的含义都传承了下来：「门达希亚，门达希亚，你用艺术的泥沙，滋养伊德莉拉的繁花。」——这就是刚刚那句歌词的意思。
t_en: Although the language of this song is no longer spoken, the meaning of each lyric has been passed down: "Mendasia, Mendasia. You nourish Idrila's flourishing flowers with the sands of art." That is the meaning of the lyrics I just sang.

t_ch: 门达希亚——在我出生之前就已毁灭的声乐之星，我从未亲眼见过它的样子，宇宙中也没有有任何关于它的影像，但只要唱起这首歌，那个能在曦光下自由放歌的天堂就会浮现在眼前。
t_en: Mendasia, the planet of canticles that perished even before I was born... I have never witnessed her or seen any images of her, but by singing that song, I can imagine a paradise where one can freely sing under the sunlight.

t_ch: …{M#先生}{F#小姐}，您觉得这首歌听起来如何呢？
t_en: ...Dear {M#sir}{F#lady}, how do you think of this song?

t_ch: 很好听。
t_en: It's beautiful.

t_ch: 谢谢您，不过我的经纪人完全不认同这种音乐。
t_en: Thank you, but my manager doesn't approve of it at all.

t_ch: 不好听。
t_en: It sounds terrible.

t_ch: 呵…您的评价还真是直接，和我的经纪人一样。
t_en: Heh heh... Your review is quite direct, just like my manager's.

t_ch: 经纪人告诉我，这种音乐风格根本就没办法吸引受众，只会把追求风雅的宾客吓跑…要想成名，就必须先唱市场认可的歌曲。
t_en: My manager told me that this style of music doesn't appeal to the audience at all and will only deter guests seeking elegance... To achieve fame, I must first sing songs that are recognized by the market.

t_ch: 于是我开始模仿流行的风格，创作虚假的「纯美之歌」。结果这居然真的骗过了听众，我的演出在乐迷圈好评如潮，我登上了《梦境艺术》的封面，虚构的歌曲也屡屡获奖……
t_en: So, I started imitating popular styles and composed the fabricated Song of Beauty. Surprisingly, that ruse managed to deceive the audience. My performances were well-received within the music fan community, I got on the cover of Art of Dreamscape, and that song even won numerous awards.

t_ch: 真是太荒唐了，最俗套的曲调配上几种冷门乐器，套上一个故事作为包装，居然就成了他们口中的艺术瑰宝。
t_en: It's absurd how the most banal melodies, coupled with some obscure instruments and a contrived narrative, can be hailed as artistic masterpieces.

t_ch: 我每天都在演唱违心的谎言，要不是刚刚一时兴起，可能都快把真正的「纯美之歌」忘掉了！
t_en: Every day, I reluctantly sing lies. If it were not for a spur-of-the-moment decision just now, I would have forgotten about the real Song of Beauty!

t_ch: 但「纯美之歌」真的一无是处吗…不，我觉得风格、受众、人气，都是掩盖成见的托词！我要向经纪人证明，门达希亚的音乐一定能大受欢迎！
t_en: However, is the Song of Beauty truly insignificant?... No, style, audience, and popularity are merely pretexts to conceal prejudices! I am determined to prove to my manager that Mendasian music will become a hit!

t_ch: 一定能大受欢迎！
t_en: It's destined to succeed!

t_ch: 谢谢您的支持，但是要将这首歌带上舞台，首先要说服那位顽固的经纪人，让她相信我的音乐不会把演出搞砸。
t_en: I appreciate your support, but before I bring this song to the stage, I must first convince my stubborn manager that my music will not blow the show.

t_ch: 至于说服她的方式…可以先进行一场路演，看看宾客们的反应。只要听众们能给出认可，那经纪人就没办法拿市场评价来当做打压我的借口了。
t_en: As for how to persuade her... I can start with a roadshow and assess the audience's reaction. If the audience approves my performance, she won't be able to use "market reviews" as an excuse to dissuade me.

t_ch: 不用证明，唱你想唱的。
t_en: You don't need to prove anything. Just sing what you desire.

t_ch: 不，没那么简单…那位经纪人把控着整个匹诺康尼的娱乐圈命脉，我必须想办法说服她，而不是反抗她。
t_en: No, it's not that simple... She holds significant sway over the entertainment industry in Penacony. I must find a way to convince her, instead of defying her.

t_ch: 至于说服她的方式…可以先进行一场路演，看看宾客们的反应。只要听众们能给出认可，那经纪人就没办法拿市场评价作为打压我的借口了。
t_en: As for how to persuade her... I can start with a roadshow and assess the audience's reaction. If the audience approves my performance, she won't be able to use "market reviews" as an excuse to dissuade me.

t_ch: 你想要怎么证明？
t_en: How are you planning to prove it?

t_ch: 我觉得可以先进行一场路演，看看宾客们的反应。只要听众们能给出认可，那经纪人就没办法拿市场评价来当做打压我的借口了。
t_en: I can start with a roadshow and assess the audience's reaction. If the audience approves my performance, she won't be able to use "market reviews" as an excuse to dissuade me.

t_ch: 路演的地点就放在艾迪恩公园吧，那里都是逐新求异的宾客，肯定能接受与众不同的音乐风格。
t_en: I can organize the roadshow at Aideen Park, as it attracts visitors who seek novelty and individuality. They'll undoubtedly be more receptive to a different style of music.

t_ch: {M#先生}{F#小姐}，我要去准备演出了。请您也来见证门达希亚的音乐在舞台上的表现吧，我的歌声一定不会让您失望的！
t_en: I'll be preparing for the show, dear {M#sir}{F#lady}. I kindly request your presence to witness my performance on stage. I'm confident that you won't be disappointed with my performance!

t_ch: 这里为啥聚着这么多人？这谁啊？
t_en: Why are so many people gathered here? Who's that woman?

t_ch: 这你都不认识？这可是来自声乐之星的拉洛丝小姐！
t_en: You don't know her? That's Larose, a renowned songstress from the planet of canticles!

t_ch: 原来是拉洛丝…不买票就能听到她的「纯美之歌」，真是赚到了！
t_en: So, it's Larose... What a treat to hear her Song of Beauty for free!

t_ch: 感谢各位宾客的捧场，这次我带来了一些特别的音乐…这些歌曲与我过去的风格不同，但我希望诸位能耐心感受一下其中的「纯美」——
t_en: Thank you all for attending, my dear audience. This time, I've brought some special music... This song is different from my usual style, but I implore your patience as you experience the Beauty within it.

t_ch: 拉洛丝献唱了真正的「纯美之歌」…
t_en: Larose sings the real Song of Beauty...

t_ch: 这…真的是拉洛丝的声音吗？
t_en: Is this... truly Larose's voice?

t_ch: 什么东西啊，太难听了……
t_en: Oh man... This is terrible...

t_ch: 不懂别乱说，拉洛丝小姐的音乐中一定别有用意……
t_en: Please stop making vulgar remarks. There must be more to Larose's music...

t_ch: 没想到我真的在大家面前唱出了「纯美之歌」，但是……
t_en: I never imagined I would perform the Song of Beauty in front of everyone, but...

t_ch: 啊，过了这么久，经纪人应该要来找我了！
t_en: Ah, it's been such a long time! My manager should be coming for me!

t_ch: 抱歉，我必须得回奥帝购物中心继续发宣传册了…亲爱的{M#先生}{F#小姐}，能麻烦您帮我问问听众的感想吗？拜托了！
t_en: I'm sorry, but I must go back to Oti Mall to distribute brochures... Dear {M#sir}{F#lady}, could you please ask about the audience's thoughts on my new song? I would greatly appreciate it!

t_ch: {M#先生}{F#小姐}，请您调查一下听众们的感想吧，这对我来说很重要……
t_en: Dear {M#sir}{F#lady}, could you please ask the audience for their thoughts? It holds great importance to me...

t_ch: 嘁，本来听说匹诺康尼有位会唱「纯美之歌」的歌手，我就毫不犹豫买了演出的门票…这下可亏大了。
t_en: Tsk... I heard that there's someone at Penacony who could sing "the Song of Beauty," so I didn't even blink when I bought the ticket for the performance... What a disappointment.

t_ch: 这种营销套路我见过太多了，先挂个虚名引来关注，再用这种离谱的行为来哗众取宠，骗取热度。如果这真是「纯美之歌」，那我洗澡时哼的曲子早就把伊德莉拉复活了！
t_en: I know all about this marketing gimmick. They create the buzz and then pull off this ridiculous act to cash in on the hype. If this so-called Song of Beauty actually worked, the tune I hum in the shower would have resurrected Idrila ages ago!

t_ch: 我得赶快想办法把票转卖出去，与其浪费时间听这种东西，不如在酒店里听知更鸟小姐的CD。
t_en: I must find a way to resell my ticket. Rather than wasting time listening to this nonsense, I'd prefer to enjoy Robin's songs at the hotel.

t_ch: 唉，变了，拉洛丝变了！这是对我们这些老乐迷的冒犯！侮辱！背叛！
t_en: What a shame... Larose has changed! It's an offense to us, her greatest fans! It's an insult! A betrayal!

t_ch: 大家喜欢的是高贵优雅的拉洛丝，用甜美的嗓音唱出柔和的歌曲…那才是纯美之歌，而不是这种恶心的杂音！
t_en: We adore the noble and elegant Larose, who sings soft songs with her sweet voice... That is the true Song of Beauty, not this repugnant mishmash!

t_ch: 真希望她快点回到之前的样子，不要再拿这些奇怪的音乐戏弄粉丝了，不然我们在乐迷圈怎么抬得起头啊……
t_en: I really wish she could return to her previous self and stop teasing her fans with such weird noises. Otherwise, how will we face our fellow music fans...

t_ch: 多么前卫的音乐…不愧是我的偶像，成名后还能潜心开拓音乐艺术的边疆，拉洛丝小姐真是位名副其实的艺术家！
t_en: What an avant-garde style of music... worthy of admiration. Larose is truly an artist in her own right, managing to be famous while pushing the boundaries of music and art!

t_ch: 这也能夸？她要不告诉我这是首歌，我就要跟猎犬家系汇报说有人在大街上发病了。
t_en: Come on! If she hadn't told me that she would sing a song, I might have reported to the Bloodhound Family that someone was making a scene on the street.

t_ch: 呵，庸俗，这叫…叫实验音乐！要的就是激进、纯粹、极端自我的表达，不懂了吧？
t_en: How putrid. This is called... experimental music! It's radical, pure, and an extreme form of self-expression, but you can't grasp it at all, can you?

t_ch: 行，我不懂，那你给我分析分析这歌好在哪？
t_en: No, I don't understand it a bit. Why don't you explain what makes this song so remarkable?

t_ch: 呃…你想啊，拉洛丝小姐为什么要在台上怪叫呢？像不像失败者们在噩梦中的哀嚎？她正是在通过声音来塑造这种情景，表达的正是逐梦客们对梦想求而不得的痛苦！
t_en: Um... Think about it... Why did Larose scream on stage? Doesn't it resemble the wailing of losers trapped in a nightmare? Through her voice, she captures that very scenario, expressing the pain felt by Dreamchasers who are unable to fulfill their dreams!

t_ch: 没听出来她唱的是不是痛苦，作为听众是挺痛苦的。
t_en: I'm not sure if she's trying to express pure pain, but it can definitely be a real pain for the audience to endure.

t_ch: 这就是艺术，让你这种俗人听出来了就不是艺术了！我看她唱的就是你这种生活不如意的人！
t_en: That's art, surpassing your mundane grasp! In my humble opinion, she was singing about people like you, whose lives aren't as fulfilling as they wish them to be.

t_ch: 亲爱的{M#先生}{F#小姐}，谢谢您刚刚的支持。请问您问到听众们的感受了吗？
t_en: Dear {M#sir}{F#lady}, thank you for your support earlier. Have you asked the audience about their thoughts?

t_ch: 褒贬不一。
t_en: Their reviews are mixed.

t_ch: 果然如此…看来我还是太天真了，经纪人说的果然没错，风格如此特别的音乐没办法轻易让大家认可。
t_en: That's understandable... I suppose I was too naive. My manager was right: Music with such a unique style is unlikely to be appreciated by everyone.

t_ch: 多半差评。
t_en: Most of the reviews are negative.

t_ch: 唉，这也是没办法的事情，对于这些欣赏流行红歌与高雅正乐的宾客来说，门达希亚的音乐无疑是主流审美的叛徒。
t_en: Unfortunately, it can't be helped. For those who appreciate popular songs, Mendasian music is undoubtedly a departure from the conventional aesthetic.

t_ch: 好、好评如潮！
t_en: M—Most of the reviews... are positive!

t_ch: 呵呵，您真是位好人，但不必如此照顾我的心情，我其实已经听到很多批评的声音了。
t_en: You are very kind, but there is no need to be overly concerned about my feelings. Actually, I have received my fair share of criticism.

t_ch: 是我太久没唱过自己喜欢的音乐了，以至于我再次唱起它的时候被快乐冲昏了头脑…其实当我站在台上的时候，就已经开始后悔了。
t_en: It's just that I haven't performed my favorite music in so long that I was overwhelmed with joy when I finally sang it again... However, I already began to regret it as soon as I stepped onto the stage.

t_ch: 我已经在匹诺康尼当了这么久的歌星，怎么会不知道市场喜欢什么样的音乐呢？开口的瞬间，我就已经猜到了结果。
t_en: I've been a famous songstress in Penacony for a long time. How could I not know what kind of music the market prefers? The moment I opened my mouth, I already foresaw the outcome.

t_ch: 我以为凭着我的名誉与粉丝，可以让人们接受真正的「纯美之歌」…但我却忘记了，这一切都是靠谎言得到的——放弃谎言，也就放弃了拥有的一切。
t_en: I thought that with my reputation and fans, I could make people appreciate the true essence of the Song of Beauty... but I forgot that I achieved everything through deception. By abandoning the falsehoods, I gave up everything I had.

t_ch: 你可以从头开始做喜欢的音乐。
t_en: You can perform your favorite music from scratch.

t_ch: 不…我做不到，也不会去做。
t_en: No... I can't, and I won't do that.

t_ch: 放弃一切没什么可怕的。
t_en: There's nothing to fear in letting go of everything.

t_ch: 亲爱的{M#先生}{F#小姐}，对我来说，没有什么事情比这更加可怕了。
t_en: But to me, dear {M#sir}{F#lady}, there are no things more terrifying than that.

t_ch: 当我在演唱结束后看到那些否定的眼神，耳边听不到一点欢呼与掌声时，我感觉自己仿佛被噩梦撕碎了一样——
t_en: At the end of my performance, when I saw those disapproving glances and heard no cheers or applause in my ears, it felt as though I was being torn apart by the most terrible nightmare...

t_ch: ——压在我身上的是无与伦比的痛苦，比母星的毁灭与纯美女神的消殒还要恐怖。
t_en: ...I experienced unimaginable pain, more agonizing than the destruction of my home planet or the demise of the Beauty.

t_ch: 那时我彻底明白了，我想要的原来不是让大家喜欢上「纯美之歌」，而是要大家喜欢上「拉洛丝」——比起门达希亚与「纯美」女神，我更爱我自己。
t_en: It was then that I fully realized that my desire was not for everyone to appreciate the Song of Beauty, but for everyone to appreciate Larose. It turns out that I love myself more than Mendasia and the Goddess of Beauty.

t_ch: 「…Mendaxia！Mendaxia！tuven arztis liberan sanam！nururisha Idrila bfularun！」
t_en: "...Mendasia! Mendasia! Tuven arztis liberan sanam! Nururisha Idrila bfularun!"

t_ch: 拉洛丝再次唱起「纯美之歌」，她的歌声像之前一样低沉嘶哑、野蛮粗糙，但不再饱含活力与喜悦，而像是一种放肆的宣泄，宣泄压抑已久的痛苦。
t_en: Larose sings the Song of Beauty once again, her voice as low, hoarse, savage, and rough as before, but no longer filled with vigor and joy. Instead, it became an unbridled catharsis of long-suppressed pain.

t_ch: {M#先生}{F#小姐}，您或许就是「纯美之歌」的最后一位听众了。以后，我会把它封藏进无人问津的角落…然后继续歌唱庸俗的谎言，在掌声与欢呼中得到满足。
t_en: Dear {M#sir}{F#lady}, I'm afraid you will be the last listener of the Song of Beauty. I will tuck it away and never perform it again... and continue singing those vulgar lies, deriving satisfaction from the applause and cheers.

t_ch: 是的，这就是真正的拉洛丝。亲爱的{M#先生}{F#小姐}，您还会愿意支持这样的我吗？
t_en: Yes, this is the real Larose. Will you continue supporting me like this, dear {M#sir}{F#lady}?

t_ch: 我还是会继续支持你的。
t_en: I'll continue supporting you.

t_ch: 呵呵，您还真是位好心的听众。
t_en: Thank you for your unwavering support. You are truly a kind listener.

t_ch: 我不会辜负您的，即便是虚假的谎言，我也会让它们更加动听。
t_en: I won't let you down. Even if it's a fabricated lie, I will make it more melodious.

t_ch: 我不会再支持你这样的歌手了。
t_en: I won't support you anymore.

t_ch: 嗯…我能理解，毕竟要用纯美女神与声乐之星的名声行骗，还要利用爱戴我的听众，这种事在您眼中很过分吧？
t_en: Well... I can understand that. After all, I'm using the reputation of the Beauty and the planet of canticles to deceive my adoring audience and take advantage of them. It's excessive in your eyes, isn't it?

t_ch: 但我已经决定了要走这样的路，如果您讨厌我，还请做好心理准备——因为我将会更加努力地出现在公众视野中，成为一名尽人皆知的歌手。
t_en: But I have made my decision. So, if you despise me, be prepared. I will work even harder to gain public attention and become a singer known by everyone.

t_ch: 无论如何，感谢您帮我从虚伪的梦中醒来…我现在找到了内心真正的渴望，并愿意为之付出一切。
t_en: Anyway, thank you for awakening me from my false dreams... Now, I have discovered my true aspirations, and I'm willing to go to any lengths to pursue them.

t_ch: 我并没有忘记初心，只是找到了自己真正想要的东西。
t_en: I haven't forgotten my true self. I've simply discovered my true passion.

t_ch: 从一开始，我想要的就是功成名就，而不是什么传承艺术的使命。
t_en: From the start, my goal was to achieve success and fame, rather than the calling of passing on the art.

t_ch: 拉洛丝的表情逐渐平和下来，笑容凝固在了嘴角，低声呢喃了起来。
t_en: Larose's expression calms down, her smile fading at the corners of her mouth as she murmurs in a low voice.

t_ch: 还记得小时候跟长辈卖唱的日子，那时我们的演出大都是为了满足人们的好奇心，让听众发现人的喉咙还能发出这么特别的声音。说是歌手，但其实和飞碟马戏团里钻火圈的扑满没什么区别。
t_en: I can still vividly recall my childhood days, singing songs with my relatives. Most of our performances were meant to satisfy people's curiosity and showcase that humans could create such unique sounds. While we called ourselves singers, we weren't much different from those Trotters who entertain with daring acts in the Cirque du Soucoupe.

t_ch: 我认为「纯美之歌」真的很美，但在无人欣赏的处境中，我也开始想要放弃歌唱…直到一场意外的星际交通事故，我和族人们被困在了一个「原始博士」残害的世界中。
t_en: At first, I genuinely believed the Song of Beauty was truly beautiful. However, when nobody appreciated it, I started contemplating giving up singing... until an interstellar traffic accident left us stranded on a world maimed by Dr. Primitive.

t_ch: 那里的居民都因为恶徒的实验变成了低智的猿猴，它们无法理解复杂的音乐，却会被我们原始而古老的歌谣吸引。
t_en: The inhabitants of that world had been transformed into apes through malicious experiments. Though they couldn't grasp complex music, they were naturally drawn to our primitive and ancient songs.

t_ch: 我在营地练歌时，这些原住民居然用残存的人智为我欢呼鼓舞，我在一群猴子的掌声中找到了继续歌唱的理由——他人的认可。
t_en: As I practiced my songs in the camp, these natives cheered me on with their remaining intelligence, and I found a reason to continue singing through their applause — validation from others.

t_ch: 是啊，艺术的追求、家乡的遗产、纯美的信仰…这都是我用来哄骗自己的空话，从一开始，我歌唱的理由便是为了听众们的追捧。
t_en: Yes, the pursuit of art, the legacy of my homeland, and the faith for the Beauty... They were all mere empty words I used to deceive myself. In truth, I sang solely for the admiration of the audience right from the start.

t_ch: 没错…我一定会成为家喻户晓的巨星，让众人跪拜在我的裙下！
t_en: Indeed... I will become a household name, a superstar, with crowds bowing at my feet!

t_ch: 到了那个时候，听众与经纪人都不能再禁锢我的歌喉，我将自由地唱出我想唱的歌曲！
t_en: When the time comes, neither my audience nor my manager will be able to confine my voice. I will have the freedom to express myself through the songs I truly desire to sing!

t_ch: 拉洛丝露出了更加自信的笑容，仿佛正站在万众瞩目的舞台上。
t_en: Larose's smile grows more confident, as if she were standing on a stage in the spotlight.

t_ch: 呼——解开了那些让我痛苦多年的心结，我终于可以为事业全力以赴了。
t_en: Phew... Now that I've untangled the knots that plagued me for years, I can finally dedicate myself completely to my career.

t_ch: 我将来会成为巨星，超越知更鸟前辈，成为宇宙中无人不爱的歌手，哪怕是一个饱嗝都能让粉丝狂热欢呼！
t_en: I will become a superstar even greater than Robin, a universally adored singer! Even a simple burp of mine will send fans into a frenzy!

t_ch: 等到那个时候…我或许就能再次唱起门达希亚的音乐，没人有资格阻拦我。而所有人都会对他们曾唾弃的艺术赞叹不已，用各种艺术理论为我辩护，奉上无数赞誉！
t_en: And when that time comes... I might just be able to sing Mendasian music again, with nobody having the right to stop me. Everyone will marvel at the art they once despised, defending me with various artistic theories and showering me with countless accolades!

t_ch: 不用肩负责任，不用顾忌市场，唱什么都能靠声名收获成功——我一定要成为这样的歌手。
t_en: Unshackled by responsibilities, unconcerned with market trends, and able to sing anything while reaping success solely based on reputation — I must become a singer like this.

t_ch: 命运真是不公，让我生在夜鹰的草窝，又让我向往阳光下的高空。
t_en: Fate has dealt me an unfair hand. I was born in the humble nest of a night hawk, only to make me yearn for the sunlit heights.

t_ch: 乘风的鸟儿随心扇动翅膀就能轻松高飞，而我想要去往同一片天空，就必须违心逆风而行……
t_en: While an eagle effortlessly soars high by riding the wind, I must defy the wind and my own desires to reach the sky...

t_ch: 拉洛丝坚定的神情褪色，你听到一声叹息，她眼眸中映出的街角霓虹闪烁了起来。
t_en: Larose's resolute expression fades, and you catch a sigh as the flickering neon lights on the street corner reflect in her eyes.
